_README: Authoritative informal<->formal mapping + the authored `decoded` readbacks for the auditor site. Hand-edited. One object per entry under `entries`, keyed by the formal Rocq name. Fields: cluster (P-phase), title, decoded (plain-English readback of the FORMAL statement), source{slug|name,url,attribution} or match_title (to auto-join the corpus ledger/classification), informal (override), status (override), specializes [ids], grounding [{name,gloss}], faithfulness, extra{file,name,kind} (for entries not auto-discovered: refutation/grounding lemmas). Regenerate registry.json with scripts/build_correspondence.py; gate with --check.

AC_kcritical3.title: ACₙ is 3-ω̄-critical
AC_kcritical3.decoded: For m ≥ 3 (so the circulant tournament ACₙ has n = 2m+1 ≥ 7 vertices), ACₙ is 3-ω̄-critical: its directed clique number ω̄(ACₙ) = 3, and deleting any single vertex leaves ω̄ = 2. This is the building block giving the infinite k = 3 family for Conjecture 5.10.
AC_kcritical3.informal: For m ≥ 3, the circulant tournament ACₙ (on n = 2m+1 ≥ 7 vertices) is 3-ω̄-critical: ω̄(ACₙ) = 3 and deleting any single vertex drops the directed clique number to 2.

AC_vertex_transitive.title: ACₙ is vertex-transitive
AC_vertex_transitive.decoded: The circulant tournament ACₙ is vertex-transitive: its automorphism group acts transitively on the vertices (for any two vertices there is a digraph automorphism — here a translation, as for any Cayley digraph — sending one to the other). This is the symmetry fact that reduces the criticality check to a single deleted vertex.
AC_vertex_transitive.informal: The circulant tournament ACₙ is vertex-transitive: its automorphism group acts transitively on the vertices.

BJY_SAD_implies_strong'.title: Bang-Jensen-Yeo SAD ⇒ K-arc-strong implies strong connectivity
BJY_SAD_implies_strong'.decoded: Re-derives, against the committed sad.v API, the edge that the Strong Arc Decomposition guaranteed by Bang-Jensen-Yeo yields a strongly connected digraph — i.e. the SAD colour class spans a strong host. Validates the actual content of the BJY-SAD ⇒ strong edge.

C3_diregular1.title: The directed triangle C3 is 1-diregular
C3_diregular1.decoded: Computes that the directed 3-cycle has out-degree and in-degree equal to 1 at every vertex, i.e. it is 1-diregular. This is a concrete value-computation witness that the diregular predicate is satisfiable on a real digraph rather than vacuous.

C3_has_seymour_vertex.title: C3 has a Seymour vertex (SNC conclusion is satisfiable)
C3_has_seymour_vertex.decoded: Exhibits a concrete vertex v of the directed triangle with outdeg v <= #|Nout2 v| (both equal 1), confirming that the conclusion of Seymour's Second Neighbourhood Conjecture is achievable — the predicate is satisfiable, not an empty class.

C3_seymour_all.title: Every vertex of C3 satisfies the Seymour inequality
C3_seymour_all.decoded: Strengthens the satisfiability check: every vertex v of the directed triangle satisfies outdeg v <= #|Nout2 v|, so the second-neighbourhood inequality of SNC is realised at all vertices of a genuine digraph, a stronger non-vacuity test of the Seymour conclusion shape.

CH_source_inhabited.title: Caccetta-Häggkvist source class is inhabited (directed triangle C₃ at r=1)
CH_source_inhabited.decoded: Exhibits the directed triangle C₃ as a concrete member of the Caccetta-Häggkvist antecedent class — nonempty, loopless, with every vertex of out-degree ≥ 1 = r — so the CH hypothesis class is non-vacuous and the implication edge transports something.

CH_triangle_realised_on_C3.title: The Caccetta-Häggkvist-triangle conclusion is realised on C₃
CH_triangle_realised_on_C3.decoded: Shows C₃ actually contains a directed cycle of length 3, i.e. the conclusion of the CH-triangle conjecture is reached on a concrete witness rather than being vacuously about an empty class.

CL1_premises_inhabited.title: CL1 hypothesis class is inhabited (6-vertex two-triangle host)
CL1_premises_inhabited.decoded: Establishes that every premise of CL1 is simultaneously satisfiable: a vertex split V = V1 + V2 with both sides >= 2, each induced side admitting a SAD, and each bridge out-cut splitting into two nonempty colour parts. The honest witness is the 6-vertex host H6 (two bidirected triangles joined by a two-way bridge), since no 2-vertex side can admit a SAD. So CL1 is neither vacuously true nor obviously false on its witness.

CL1_statement.title: CL1 — bilateral controlled-lifting for Strong Arc Decompositions
CL1_statement.decoded: For every digraph D and every vertex set V1 (with V2 its complement) such that both sides have at least 2 vertices, if each induced subdigraph D[V1] and D[V2] admits a Strong Arc Decomposition, and each of the two bridge out-cuts — the arcs leaving V1 (outcut V1) and the arcs leaving V2 (outcut V2) — can be split into two disjoint, nonempty, covering arc parts, then D itself admits a Strong Arc Decomposition. A relative (SAD-from-SAD) lifting target, provable without resolving WC3 or the general Bang-Jensen–Yeo conjecture.

source.attribution: library-internal lifting lemma toward Bang-Jensen–Yeo SAD

CL1_statement.faithfulness: Each side needs a SAD, and the smallest SAD-admitting digraph is the bidirected triangle (no 2-vertex digraph has a SAD), so the non-vacuity witness uses 3-vertex sides, not 2-vertex ones.

Delta_star_C3.title: Degreewidth of the directed triangle is 1
Delta_star_C3.decoded: Computes Δ*(C₃) = 1 for the directed triangle C₃: every vertex order leaves at least one back-arc, and the identity order leaves exactly one, giving back-arc max-degree 1. This is the marquee non-transitive value of degreewidth, validating the Delta_star definition (the smallest non-sparse tournament, with Δ* > 0) underlying the matching-FAS = degreewidth-≤-1 fact.

Delta_star_C3_le2_via_LFO.title: C₃ consistency cross-check: has_LFO forces Δ*(C₃) ≤ 2
Delta_star_C3_le2_via_LFO.decoded: Cross-checks the two definitions on C₃: feeding has_LFO_C3 through the proved reduction has_LFO_Delta_star_le2 yields Δ*(C₃) ≤ 2, consistent with the exact computed value Δ*(C₃) = 1. Confirms the Path-FAS/LFO and degreewidth definitions agree on a concrete witness.

Delta_star_TT.title: Degreewidth of a transitive tournament is 0
Delta_star_TT.decoded: Computes Δ*(TTₙ) = 0 for every transitive tournament: its identity (acyclic) order has an edgeless back-arc graph, so no order leaves any back-arc. The extreme sparse case that grounds the Delta_star definition (a transitive tournament is the maximally sparse tournament).

Digon2_not_tournament.title: The digon is not a tournament (hero ceiling)
Digon2_not_tournament.decoded: Refutation probe: the two-vertex digon (arcs both ways) fails asymmetry, so it is not a tournament and hence not a hero. The second sanity ceiling confirming the hero predicate is discriminating.

E2_no_dicycle.title: The edgeless digraph E₂ has no dicycle
E2_no_dicycle.decoded: The edgeless digraph E₂ contains no directed cycle, so 'has a dicycle' is discriminating (true on C₃, false on E₂) — the packing edges' (Bermond-Thomassen / Hoang-Reed at k=1) conclusion is not a triviality.

E2_no_triangle.title: The edgeless digraph E₂ has no directed triangle
E2_no_triangle.decoded: The edgeless two-vertex digraph E₂ contains no directed 3-cycle, so 'has a directed triangle' is a discriminating property (true on C₃, false on E₂) — the CH-triangle edge's conclusion is not a triviality that every digraph satisfies.

E2_not_strongb.title: The edgeless digraph E₂ is not strongly connected
E2_not_strongb.decoded: The two-vertex edgeless digraph E₂ is not strongly connected (its vertices cannot inter-reach), so strong connectivity is a discriminating target (true on C₃, false on E₂) — the SAD edges' conclusion is not a triviality.

Edgeless2_not_tournament.title: The edgeless 2-vertex digraph is not a tournament (hero ceiling)
Edgeless2_not_tournament.decoded: Refutation probe: the two-vertex digraph with no arcs fails semicompleteness, so it is not a tournament and (since heroes are tournaments) cannot be a hero. A red-flag ceiling showing [hero] is not vacuously 'every digraph'.

LFO_iff_34transversal_statement.title: Linear-forest ordering = 3/4-cycle transversal (the equivalence target)
LFO_iff_34transversal_statement.decoded: For every tournament T, T has a linear-forest ordering if and only if there exists an arc set F whose underlying simple graph is a linear forest (acyclic, every vertex of degree at most 2) AND which hits every directed 3-cycle and every directed 4-cycle (every directed triangle and every directed 4-cycle uses some arc of F). Note F is only required to meet 3- and 4-cycles here — a strictly weaker hitting requirement than a full feedback arc set. Stated as a Prop target; the characterization is open.

source.name: arXiv 2402.10782
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Lopes

Loop1_not_dicol2.title: A single looped vertex is not 2-dicolourable
Loop1_not_dicol2.decoded: The one-vertex digraph with a self-loop is not 2-dicolourable, so dicolourability by 2 colours is discriminating — the twin-width chain's conj_3_12 target (χ⃗ ≤ 2) is not a triviality satisfied by every digraph.

Nout2_disjoint_Nout.title: Second out-neighbourhood is disjoint from the out-neighbourhood
Nout2_disjoint_Nout.decoded: Proves that Nout2 v (vertices at directed distance exactly two) is disjoint from Nout v (distance-one out-neighbours). A correctness probe of the Nout2 definition underlying Seymour's conjecture: a definition where these overlapped would be wrong.

SAD_BT3.title: Bidirected triangle BT3 has a Strong Arc Decomposition
SAD_BT3.decoded: Exhibits a SAD of the bidirected triangle BT3 (a fresh 3-element carrier): its 6 arcs split into the forward triangle 0->1->2->0 and the backward triangle 0->2->1->0, each a spanning dicycle hence spanning-strong. The smallest honest SAD witness (a 2-vertex digon has none), making SAD a satisfiable predicate.

SAD_Bicyc.title: Bidirected n-cycle has a Strong Arc Decomposition
SAD_Bicyc.decoded: Exhibits a concrete Strong Arc Decomposition of the bidirected n-cycle Bicyc n (n >= 3): its arcs split into the forward directed Hamilton cycle i -> i+1 and the backward one i -> i-1, each spanning and strongly connected. This is the smallest nontrivial witness that SAD is a satisfiable predicate, so the Bang-Jensen-Yeo / WC3 nodes do not quantify SAD over an empty class.

SAD_colouring_Bicyc.title: Bidirected n-cycle has a SAD via the 2-colouring presentation
SAD_colouring_Bicyc.decoded: Re-proves the SAD of the bidirected n-cycle through the colouring form SAD_colouring (an arc is coloured by whether it is a forward arc), validating that the colouring presentation of SAD agrees with the arc-set-partition presentation on a concrete witness.

SAD_complete3.title: Every 3-vertex bidirected (complete-symmetric) digraph has a SAD
SAD_complete3.decoded: Generic grounding: any digraph on 3 vertices whose arc relation is inequality (the bidirected triangle) has a Strong Arc Decomposition, its 6 arcs splitting into the two oriented Hamilton 3-cycles. Provides the reusable smallest-SAD building block underpinning the CL1 non-vacuity witness.

SAD_predicate_inhabited.title: SAD is a satisfiable predicate
SAD_predicate_inhabited.decoded: States the existence of a nonempty digraph with a Strong Arc Decomposition (witnessed by BT3). Confirms bang_jensen_yeo_SAD_statement and WC3_statement do not quantify SAD D over an empty class.

T4_kcritical4.title: T4 = ACₙ[C₃] is 4-ω̄-critical
T4_kcritical4.decoded: The tournament T4 (the lexicographic product ACₙ[C₃], for n = 2m+1 with m ≥ 3) is 4-ω̄-critical: kcritical 4 T4 unfolds to ω̄(T4) = 4 and, for EVERY vertex v, ω̄(T4 − v) = 4.-1 = 3. This is the single-witness criticality fact (the building block); the arbitrarily-large family statement of Conjecture 5.10 is conjecture_5_10_at_k4, not this lemma.
T4_kcritical4.informal: The tournament T4 = ACₙ[C₃] is 4-ω̄-critical: ω̄(T4) = 4 and deleting any single vertex drops the directed clique number to 3.

T5_kcritical5.title: AACL Conj 5.10 — the host T₅ is 5-ω̄-critical
T5_kcritical5.decoded: The tournament T₅ = ACₙ[ACₙ] (the lexicographic product of the circulant ACₙ with itself, for odd n = 2m+1 with m ≥ 3) is 5-ω̄-critical: its directed clique number ω̄(T₅) equals 5, and deleting any single vertex v drops it to ω̄(T₅ − v) = 4.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, 2023)

TT2_not_SAD.title: The single-arc digraph TT 2 has no SAD
TT2_not_SAD.decoded: A discriminating probe: the transitive tournament TT 2 (one arc 0 -> 1) has NO Strong Arc Decomposition, since vertex 1 is a sink and no arc set on it can be spanning-strong. Confirms SAD is a non-trivial property, not satisfied by every digraph, so the SAD conjecture nodes are not vacuously true.

TT_has_sink.title: Transitive tournament TT(n+1) has a sink of in-degree n
TT_has_sink.decoded: Proves that in the transitive tournament TT(n+1) the greatest vertex ord_max is beaten by every other vertex, so indeg ord_max = n. The dual value-computation witness for the indeg encoding on the canonical transitive tournament.

TT_has_source.title: Transitive tournament TT(n+1) has a source of out-degree n
TT_has_source.decoded: Proves that in the transitive tournament TT(n+1) (arcs u-->v iff u<v) the least vertex ord0 beats every other vertex, so outdeg ord0 = n. A key value computation validating the outdeg encoding on the canonical transitive tournament, and a probe showing degrees can be as large as n-1.

WC3_implies_3arcstrong_strong'.title: WC3 ⇒ every nonempty 3-arc-strong digraph is strongly connected
WC3_implies_3arcstrong_strong'.decoded: Re-derives the WC3 ⇒ strong edge: under WC3, the SAD of any nonempty 3-arc-strong digraph spans a strong host, so the digraph is strongly connected. Validates the WC3 ⇒ strong-connectivity edge.

WC3_statement.title: Bang-Jensen–Yeo SAD at K = 3 (working conjecture WC3)
WC3_statement.decoded: Every nonempty digraph D (0 < #|D|) that is 3-arc-strong admits a Strong Arc Decomposition, i.e. its arc set partitions into two disjoint arc classes that each span all vertices and are each strongly connected. This is the K = 3 instance of the Bang-Jensen–Yeo SAD conjecture: if true it settles that conjecture with K = 3, and a 3-arc-strong counterexample would refute it.

source.attribution: Bang-Jensen, Yeo (K = 3 form)

acyclic_number_ge1.title: Every oriented digraph has acyclic number ≥ 1
acyclic_number_ge1.decoded: Proves any oriented digraph has acyclic_number_ge D 1 (a single vertex induces an acyclic subdigraph); this is exactly why avec_core_statement was trivially satisfiable by the constant g≡1 binder, the RF2 defect that prompted pinning g to the true minimum a⃗(n).

acyclicb_TT.title: Transitive tournament is acyclic (χ⃗(TTₙ) = 1)
acyclicb_TT.decoded: Proves the transitive tournament TT n is acyclic, since arcs force vertex values to strictly increase along every path. This is the textbook value χ⃗(TTₙ) = 1 and a sanity witness that acyclicb is satisfiable on a nontrivial tournament.

arc_strong_C3_1.title: Directed triangle C3 is 1-arc-strong
arc_strong_C3_1.decoded: Proves arc_strong C3 1: every nonempty proper out-cut of the directed triangle has size >= 1. Grounds arc_strong / outcut on the canonical strongly connected example.

avec_antecedent_TT2.title: a⃗/t⃗ core antecedent is inhabited (TT 2)
avec_antecedent_TT2.decoded: Proves the single arc TT 2 is nonempty, oriented, and underlying-triangle-free — the full antecedent of the a⃗/t⃗ combinatorial cores. This non-vacuity witness shows the oriented triangle-free class of Conjectures 3 and 4 (arXiv:2403.02298) is inhabited.

avec_core_statement.title: Minimum acyclic number of oriented triangle-free graphs — combinatorial core (a⃗)
avec_core_statement.decoded: There is a sequence g : nat -> nat such that, for every order n > 0: (lower bound) every digon-free digraph D on exactly n vertices whose underlying graph is triangle-free has an acyclic induced subdigraph of size at least g(n); and (attainment) some such order-n graph has no acyclic induced set of size g(n)+1, which pins g(n) to the exact minimum acyclic number a⃗(n). This is the combinatorial core of the Θ(√(n·log n)) conjecture, with the asymptotic envelope abstracted into the existence of such a binding sequence g.

source.name: arXiv 2403.02298
source.attribution: Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al.

bang_jensen_yeo_SAD_statement.title: Bang-Jensen–Yeo Strong Arc Decomposition Conjecture
bang_jensen_yeo_SAD_statement.decoded: There is an absolute constant K such that every nonempty digraph D (0 < #|D|) that is K-arc-strong admits a Strong Arc Decomposition — a partition of its arc set into two disjoint arc classes, each spanning all vertices and each strongly connected (equivalently, a pair of arc-disjoint strongly connected spanning subdigraphs). The constant K is existentially bound, so the statement asserts that some finite arc-strength threshold forces such a decomposition. (Known: K = 2 fails — infinite obstruction families exist; no 3-arc-strong obstruction is known.)

source.attribution: Bang-Jensen, Yeo (2004)

bermond_thomassen_antecedent_C3.title: Bermond-Thomassen antecedent is inhabited at k = 1
bermond_thomassen_antecedent_C3.decoded: Shows C3 is nonempty and every vertex has out-degree >= 2*1-1, so the now-guarded Bermond-Thomassen antecedent is satisfied by C3. Confirms the 0 < #|D| guard fix yields a non-empty hypothesis class (excludes only the empty-digraph counterexample).

bermond_thomassen_k1_holds.title: Bermond-Thomassen holds at k = 1 on nonempty digraphs
bermond_thomassen_k1_holds.decoded: The k = 1 slice of Bermond-Thomassen is a theorem: every nonempty digraph of min out-degree >= 1 has a vertex-disjoint cycle packing of size 1. Confirms the (guarded) bermond_thomassen_statement is correctly calibrated at k = 1.

bermond_thomassen_statement.title: Bermond–Thomassen Conjecture
bermond_thomassen_statement.decoded: For every nonempty digraph D (0 < #|D|) and every k, if every vertex has out-degree at least 2k−1 (pointwise, forall v: 2*k−1 ≤ outdeg v), then D contains a packing of k directed cycles that are pairwise vertex-disjoint: there is a list P of seqs, each a directed cycle (cycle_pack P), with all members pairwise sharing no vertex (vtx_disjoint_pack P) and size P = k.

bicyc_arc_strong.title: Bidirected n-cycle is 2-arc-strong
bicyc_arc_strong.decoded: Proves arc_strong (Bicyc n) 2 for n >= 3: every nonempty proper out-cut has a forward and a backward boundary arc (distinct by s x != p x), so its size is at least 2. Grounds arc_strong at a value strictly above 1 and supplies the inhabited hypothesis-class witness for the SAD conjectures, where the premise is k-arc-strong and the conclusion (SAD) is achievable.

caccetta_haggkvist_statement.title: Caccetta–Häggkvist Conjecture
caccetta_haggkvist_statement.decoded: For every loopless digraph D on n vertices (0 < #|D|) in which every vertex has out-degree at least r, with r ≥ 1, there is a directed cycle of length at most ⌈n/r⌉ (written as (n+r−1) ÷ r). The nonemptiness guard 0 < #|D|, the looplessness condition (no vertex v has an arc v --> v), and r ≥ 1 are all stated explicitly.

caccetta_haggkvist_triangle_statement.title: Caccetta–Häggkvist Conjecture — triangle case (r = n/3)
caccetta_haggkvist_triangle_statement.decoded: The famous triangle special case, over oriented (digon-free) digraphs: for every nonempty oriented digraph D (0 < #|D|) in which every vertex has out-degree at least n/3 — encoded as n ≤ 3·outdeg(v) for all v — there is a directed cycle of length exactly 3 (a directed triangle).

canon_tree_legal.title: The Def-9.1 base plane-tree datum is legal
canon_tree_legal.decoded: Proves is_two_hajos_data holds for canon_tree (a root with two B-leaves: ≥2 edges, even-B-parity, ≥1 leaf, no illegal A-blocks), so the legality predicate is inhabited and the tree-join generator inH2_treejoin is not vacuous.

card_AC.title: ACₙ has n vertices
card_AC.decoded: The circulant tournament ACₙ has exactly n vertices (n = 2m+1, the order of the underlying cyclic group ℤ/nℤ).
card_AC.informal: The circulant tournament ACₙ has exactly n vertices.

card_T4.title: Order of T4 = ACₙ[C₃] is 3n
card_T4.decoded: The tournament T4 = ACₙ[C₃] (with n = 2m+1, m ≥ 3) has exactly 3n vertices (#|T4| = n·3), since ACₙ has n vertices and C₃ has 3 and the lexicographic product multiplies orders.
card_T4.informal: The tournament T4 = ACₙ[C₃] has exactly 3n vertices.

card_T5.title: AACL Conj 5.10 — order of the host T₅ is n²
card_T5.decoded: The host tournament T₅ = ACₙ[ACₙ] has exactly n² vertices (n = 2m+1), since the lexicographic product of two ACₙ's, each of order n, has n·n vertices. This is what makes distinct n yield arbitrarily large critical tournaments.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, 2023)

ch_hyp_C3.title: C3 satisfies the Caccetta-Häggkvist hypotheses (non-vacuity witness)
ch_hyp_C3.decoded: Verifies that the directed triangle meets all antecedents of the Caccetta-Häggkvist statement: positive order, looplessness, r=1, and minimum out-degree at least 1. This shows the universally-quantified CH statement is not vacuous — it genuinely quantifies over a nonempty class of digraphs.

ch_triangle_hyp_C3.title: C3 satisfies the CH triangle-case hypothesis (non-vacuity witness)
ch_triangle_hyp_C3.decoded: Verifies that the directed triangle meets the triangle-case antecedent forall v, #|C3| <= 3 * outdeg v (3 <= 3*1), so the CH triangle-case (r = n/3) statement is satisfiable by a genuine instance: the universally-quantified statement is not vacuous. (C3 also happens to contain a directed triangle, consistent with — but not proved by — this hypothesis check.)

cheng_keevash_conj1_statement.title: Cheng–Keevash Conjecture 1 — directed path of length 2·δ⁺
cheng_keevash_conj1_statement.decoded: For every oriented digraph D (loopless, digon-free) with 0 < #|D| and every d such that every vertex has out-degree at least d (d ≤ outdeg v for all v), the longest directed path of D has length at least 2d (2·d ≤ ell D, where ell D is the number of arcs on a longest dipath). Equivalently: minimum out-degree ≥ d forces a directed path with 2d arcs.
cheng_keevash_conj1_statement.match_title: Directed path of length twice the minimum outdegree

chi_bounded_bounded_order.title: Any bounded-order class is χ-bounded (wrapper satisfiable)
chi_bounded_bounded_order.decoded: Proves any class of digraphs of order at most N is χ-bounded by the constant N, since χ(underlying G) ≤ #|G| ≤ N. This shows the chi_bounded_under wrapper is genuinely satisfiable, so the open χ-boundedness conjectures are not trivially refutable.

chi_bounded_empty.title: The empty class is vacuously χ-bounded
chi_bounded_empty.decoded: Proves the empty digraph class is χ-bounded, pinning the wrapper at its degenerate pole (it says nothing). Together with the bounded-order witness this shows chi_bounded_under is non-degenerate, so no encoding accident makes the open conjectures provable for free.

chi_dcycle_C4.title: χ⃗(directed C₄) = 2 — heroes tightness witness
chi_dcycle_C4.decoded: Specialises χ⃗ = 2 to the directed C₄, the canonical tightness witness for the Forb_ind(digon, C₃, S₂⁺) χ⃗ ≤ 2 bound: C₄ is oriented, C₃-free and S₂⁺-free yet attains the bound. This shows the conjectured bound is exactly attained, not merely vacuously satisfied.

chi_dcycle_eq2.title: χ⃗(directed n-cycle) = 2 for n ≥ 2
chi_dcycle_eq2.decoded: Headline value computation: the directed n-cycle is not 1-dicolourable (χ⃗ ≥ 2) yet 2-dicolourable (χ⃗ ≤ 2), so its dichromatic number is exactly 2. This extends the C₃ keystone to the whole family of directed cycles, validating dicolorableb across an infinite family.

chordal_C3_C3.title: C₃ lies in the chordal directed-graph class
chordal_C3_C3.decoded: Proves the directed triangle is oriented, has no induced transitive TT₃, and no induced long dicycle, so it inhabits the chordal_C3 class of arXiv:2202.01006. This non-vacuity witness shows the class over which the not-χ⃗-bounded statement ranges is inhabited.

chordal_C3_not_TT3.title: TT₃ is NOT in the chordal class (class is proper)
chordal_C3_not_TT3.decoded: Red-flag probe: the transitive tournament TT₃ contains an induced transitive triangle, so it is excluded from chordal_C3. This shows the chordal class genuinely separates objects (neither empty nor everything), so the not-χ⃗-bounded statement is not vacuously about a degenerate class.

chordal_not_dichromatic_bounded_statemen.title: Chordal directed graphs are not χ⃗-bounded
chordal_not_dichromatic_bounded_statemen.decoded: It is NOT the case that the chordal class C₃ is dichromatic-bounded: there is no single constant B such that every digraph in C₃ is B-dicolourable (χ⃗ ≤ B). Here C₃ is the class of digon-free digraphs with no induced transitive triangle TT₃ and no induced (chordless) directed cycle of length ≥ 4, so the dichromatic number is unbounded over this class.

source.name: arXiv 2202.01006
source.attribution: Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al. (2022)

ck_conj1_delta2.title: Cheng–Keevash Conj 1 (Thomassé directed-path) — proved at δ = 2
ck_conj1_delta2.decoded: For every nonempty oriented graph D (0 < #|D|, no loops, no digons) in which every vertex has out-degree at least 2, the longest directed simple path has length at least 4 (ℓ(D) ≥ 4). This is the δ = 2 case of the conjecture that minimum out-degree δ forces a directed path of length 2δ, proved unconditionally as a corollary of the general Lemma 7 / Theorem 4 bound.
ck_conj1_delta2.match_title: Directed path of length twice the minimum outdegree

ck_conj1_delta3.title: Cheng–Keevash Conj 1 (Thomassé directed-path) — proved at δ = 3
ck_conj1_delta3.decoded: For every nonempty oriented graph D (0 < #|D|, no loops, no digons) in which every vertex has out-degree at least 3, the longest directed simple path has length at least 6 (ℓ(D) ≥ 6); ℓ(D) counts arcs. This is the δ = 3 case of the conjecture that minimum out-degree δ forces a directed path of length 2δ, proved unconditionally.
ck_conj1_delta3.match_title: Directed path of length twice the minimum outdegree

ck_conj1_delta3_path.title: Cheng–Keevash Conj 1 at δ = 3 — explicit 6-arc path
ck_conj1_delta3_path.decoded: For every nonempty oriented graph D (0 < #|D|, no loops, no digons) in which every vertex has out-degree at least 3, there exist a start vertex x and a sequence s of 6 vertices forming a directed simple path from x (dipath x s with size s = 6); i.e. a directed simple path with 6 arcs exists. This is the path-existence (unfolded) form of the δ = 3 case of the directed-path conjecture.
ck_conj1_delta3_path.match_title: Directed path of length twice the minimum outdegree

ck_theorem4_oriented.title: Cheng–Keevash Theorem 4 (oriented) — ℓ(D) ≥ 2δ − ⌊(δ−1)/2⌋
ck_theorem4_oriented.decoded: For every nonempty oriented graph D (0 < #|D|, no loops, no digons) and every δ such that every vertex has out-degree at least δ, the longest directed simple path satisfies ℓ(D) ≥ 2δ − ⌊(δ−1)/2⌋ (nat truncated subtraction; the ⌊·⌋/2 coded as (δ−1)./2). This is the general unconditional lower bound; at δ = 2 it gives the 2δ = 4 conjecture bound exactly (2·2 − ⌊1/2⌋ = 4), and it is the partial-progress envelope toward the full 2δ directed-path conjecture.
ck_theorem4_oriented.match_title: Directed path of length twice the minimum outdegree

clique_forward_chain.title: Forward clique chain: Question 5.9 ⇒ domination-ω̄ cluster
clique_forward_chain.decoded: Composes the committed edges Q5.9 ⇒ 5.8 ⇒ dom-cluster, confirming transitivity lands on the domination-ω̄ cluster statement.

conj2_1605_statement.title: Aboulker–Charbit–Naserasr Conjecture 2 — Forb(H) is χ-bounded iff underlying H is a forest
conj2_1605_statement.decoded: For every digon-free (oriented) graph H: the class of oriented graphs that contain no induced copy of H is χ-bounded if and only if the underlying undirected graph of H is a forest. Here χ-bounded means a single binding function f bounds the ordinary undirected chromatic number χ(underlying G) ≤ f(ω(underlying G)) for every nonempty member G of the class.
conj2_1605_statement.match_title: Conjecture 2 (1605.07411): Forb(H) chi-bounded iff H is a forest

source.name: arXiv 1605.07411
source.attribution: Aboulker, Charbit, Naserasr

conj2_antecedent_inhabited.title: Conjecture 2 antecedent (oriented_dg) is inhabited
conj2_antecedent_inhabited.decoded: Exhibits C₃ as a concrete oriented digraph, so the oriented_dg hypothesis class of Aboulker-Charbit-Naserasr Conjecture 2 is non-empty. A forall over an empty antecedent class would say nothing; this confirms the statement has content.

conj2_specialises_tournament.title: Majority 3-colouring specialises to tournaments
conj2_specialises_tournament.decoded: Re-exposes majority_3col_statement -> majority_3col_tournament_statement as an honest specialisation, confirming the tournament version is a genuine restriction of the general majority 3-colouring conjecture.

conj3_avec_Theta_statement.title: Conjecture 3 (Aboulker–Bousquet et al.) — a⃗(n) = Θ(√(n·ln n))
conj3_avec_Theta_statement.decoded: For every nat-sequence av that genuinely equals the extremal minimum acyclic number a⃗ (is_avec av: for each order n > 0, av n is both a lower bound on the acyclic number of every n-vertex oriented triangle-free digraph and attained by some such digraph), the real sequence n ↦ av n is Θ of env_a(n) = √(n·ln n). Θ is the two-sided Landau predicate: there exist positive reals c₁, c₂ and a threshold N with c₁·√(n·ln n) ≤ av n ≤ c₂·√(n·ln n) for all n ≥ N.

source.name: arXiv 2403.02298
source.attribution: Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al.

conj3_avec_exists_statement.title: Conjecture 3 (Aboulker–Bousquet et al.) — existential form: a⃗ exists and is Θ(√(n·ln n))
conj3_avec_exists_statement.decoded: There exists a nat-sequence av that genuinely equals the extremal minimum acyclic number a⃗ (is_avec av) AND is Θ of env_a(n) = √(n·ln n) — i.e. there are positive reals c₁, c₂ and a threshold N with c₁·√(n·ln n) ≤ av n ≤ c₂·√(n·ln n) eventually. A concrete-witness reading equivalent in content to the universally-quantified form.

source.name: arXiv 2403.02298
source.attribution: Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al.

conj4_1605_statement.title: Aboulker–Charbit–Naserasr Conjecture 4 — every oriented star is χ-bounding
conj4_1605_statement.decoded: For every oriented star S (an orientation of K_{1,t}: one centre adjacent in the underlying graph to all others, with the leaves pairwise non-adjacent), the class of oriented graphs with no induced copy of S is χ-bounded — a single binding function bounds χ(underlying G) ≤ f(ω(underlying G)) for every nonempty member. A special case of Conjecture 2, since an oriented star is an oriented forest.
conj4_1605_statement.match_title: Conjecture 4 (1605.07411): Forb(S) chi-bounded for every oriented star S

source.name: arXiv 1605.07411
source.attribution: Aboulker, Charbit, Naserasr

conj4_antecedent_inhabited.title: Conjecture 4 antecedent (oriented_star) is inhabited
conj4_antecedent_inhabited.decoded: Exhibits the single arc TT 2 as an oriented star (orientation of K_{1,1}), so the oriented_star hypothesis class of Conjecture 4 is non-empty. This non-vacuity check ensures the χ-bounding-star statement is not over an empty class.

conj4_tvec_Theta_statement.title: Conjecture 4 (Aboulker–Bousquet et al.) — t⃗(n) = Θ(√(n/ln n))
conj4_tvec_Theta_statement.decoded: For every nat-sequence tv that genuinely equals the extremal maximum dichromatic number t⃗ (is_tvec tv: for each order n > 0, every n-vertex oriented triangle-free digraph is tv n-dicolourable, χ⃗ ≤ tv n, and this is attained by some such digraph), the real sequence n ↦ tv n is Θ of env_t(n) = √(n/ln n). Θ here means there exist positive reals c₁, c₂ and a threshold N with c₁·√(n/ln n) ≤ tv n ≤ c₂·√(n/ln n) for all n ≥ N.

source.name: arXiv 2403.02298
source.attribution: Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al.

conj4_tvec_exists_statement.title: Conjecture 4 (Aboulker–Bousquet et al.) — existential form: t⃗ exists and is Θ(√(n/ln n))
conj4_tvec_exists_statement.decoded: There exists a nat-sequence tv that genuinely equals the extremal maximum dichromatic number t⃗ (is_tvec tv) AND is Θ of env_t(n) = √(n/ln n) — i.e. there are positive reals c₁, c₂ and a threshold N with c₁·√(n/ln n) ≤ tv n ≤ c₂·√(n/ln n) eventually. A concrete-witness reading equivalent in content to the universally-quantified form.

source.name: arXiv 2403.02298
source.attribution: Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al.

conj5_1605_statement.title: Aboulker–Charbit–Naserasr Conjecture 5 — non-exceptional families of P₄-orientations are χ-bounding
conj5_1605_statement.decoded: For every family forb of orientations of the 4-vertex path P₄ that is nonempty (some member exists) and contains a member that is neither the directed path →P₄ nor the alternating orientation P⁺(1,1,1) — which rules out both exceptional singletons — the class of oriented graphs with no induced copy of any member of forb is χ-bounded (a single f bounds χ(underlying G) ≤ f(ω(underlying G)) for every nonempty member).
conj5_1605_statement.match_title: Conjecture 5 (1605.07411): Forb of subsets of Or(P4) chi-bounded except two exceptions

source.name: arXiv 1605.07411
source.attribution: Aboulker, Charbit, Naserasr

conj5_antecedent_inhabited.title: Conjecture 5 antecedent (P4_underlying) is inhabited
conj5_antecedent_inhabited.decoded: Builds the all-forward directed path dP4 (0→1→2→3), proves it realises is_dirP4, and concludes the P4_underlying hypothesis class of Conjecture 5 is non-empty. This confirms the P₄-orientation conjecture ranges over a genuine class.

conj9_implies_conj2_edge.title: Conj 9 (fractional majority) implies Conj 2 (majority 3-colouring)
conj9_implies_conj2_edge.decoded: Re-exposes the committed implication majority_k1col_statement -> majority_3col_statement as a clean, content-bearing edge, confirming the fractional majority conjecture specialises to the majority 3-colouring conjecture and the encodings are faithfully linked.

conj_3_12_class_inhabited.title: The antecedent class of Conjecture 3.12 is inhabited for every k
conj_3_12_class_inhabited.decoded: For every k exhibits a nonempty tournament T with 0 < #|T| and tww_le T k (namely TT 1), proving the antecedent of conj_3_12_statement is satisfiable for each k so the conjecture is not a vacuous quantification over an empty class.

conj_3_12_has_teeth.title: Conjecture 3.12 has teeth on the concrete tournament TT 1
conj_3_12_has_teeth.decoded: Falsification probe: assuming conj_3_12_statement, any binding function it provides forces TT 1 to be m-dicolourable for some m on the real datum ω̄(TT 1)=1, showing the statement is not secretly vacuous (a misencoded True would impose no such constraint).

conj_3_12_statement.title: AACL Conjecture 3.12 — bounded-twin-width tournaments are ω̄-bounded

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments)

conj_3_12_statement.decoded: For every k there is a binding function f_k (the existential ∃f sits inside the ∀k, so f may depend on k) such that every nonempty tournament T (0 < #|T|) whose twin-width is at most k (tww_le T k) is f_k(ω̄(T))-dicolourable, i.e. χ⃗(T) ≤ f_k(ω̄(T)) — dicolorableb T m encodes exactly χ⃗(T) ≤ m. In other words, within each bounded-twin-width class the dichromatic number is bounded by a function of the tournament clique number ω̄.

conj_3_13_statement.title: AACL Conjecture 3.13 — one ordering bounding both backedge clique number and ordered twin-width

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments)

conj_3_13_statement.decoded: There is a single function f (one ∃f over all tournaments) such that every nonempty tournament T (0 < #|T|) admits an ordering p of its vertices that simultaneously bounds two quantities by f(ω̄(T)): the backedge clique number ω(T^p) (bclique p ≤ f(ω̄(T))) AND the ordered twin-width of that ordering (otww_le p (f(ω̄(T)))). Both bounds use the same value f(ω̄(T)).

conj_3_16_statement.title: AACL Conjecture 3.16 — a BST-ordering bounding the backedge clique number

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments)

conj_3_16_statement.decoded: There is a single function f (one ∃f over all tournaments) such that every nonempty tournament T (0 < #|T|) admits an ordering p that is a BST-ordering (bst_order p) whose backedge clique number ω(T^p) is at most f(ω̄(T)) (bclique p ≤ f(ω̄(T))).

conj_4_2_RHS_both_nonvacuous.title: Both disjuncts of the conj_4_2 dichotomy RHS are non-vacuous
conj_4_2_RHS_both_nonvacuous.decoded: Proves the hero-dichotomy RHS quantifies over real objects on both sides: Empty2's underlying graph is a (star) forest AND TT 2 is a transitive tournament. Guards the biconditional against a vacuous disjunction.

conj_5_10_and_Q5_9_incompatible.title: Conjecture 5.10 and Question 5.9 are mutually exclusive
conj_5_10_and_Q5_9_incompatible.decoded: From the committed refuting edge (5.10 ⇒ ¬Q5.9), proves Conjecture 5.10 and Question 5.9 cannot both hold; this guarantees the clique chain never loops back into a contradiction even though one branch refutes Q5.9 and another assumes it.

conj_6_2_witness.title: Empty2 witnesses the full conj_6_2 antecedent and conclusion
conj_6_2_witness.decoded: Packages that Empty2 is oriented, C₃-free, S₂⁺-free AND 2-dicolourable, so the conj_6_2 forall-statement has a real witness on which it holds and its antecedent class is non-empty (thm_6_1_witness does the same for the →K₂+K₁-free variant).

conj_9_2_both_sides_nonvacuous.title: The H₂ side of the Conjecture 9.2 biconditional is inhabited
conj_9_2_both_sides_nonvacuous.decoded: Shows the H₂ class is inhabited by both the symmetric triangle and the wheel W₃ (each satisfies in_H2_concrete realises_gw), so the right-hand side of the AACL Conjecture 9.2 biconditional (two_extremal <-> in_H2) instantiated at in_H2_concrete realises_gw ranges over real objects rather than an empty class. This grounds only the in_H2 side; it does not exhibit a two_extremal witness.

conj_9_2_gw_H2_nonvacuous.title: The wheel-realisation H₂ side of conj_9_2 is non-vacuous
conj_9_2_gw_H2_nonvacuous.decoded: Proves [in_H2_concrete realises_gw] is inhabited by both the symmetric-triangle base and the wheel gwheel 3, so the H₂ side of the conj_9_2_concrete biconditional under the wheel realisation quantifies over real objects (companion lemmas do the same for realises_W and realises_E).

conj_9_antecedent_inhabited.title: Acyclic digraph with > 1 vertex exists (conj_9 antecedent)
conj_9_antecedent_inhabited.decoded: Exhibits TT 2 as an acyclic digraph with at least 2 vertices, so deleting a vertex leaves something. Confirms the antecedent class of Conjecture 9 (acyclic D with 1 < #|D|) is inhabited and its universal is not over an empty class. (Conjecture 9 — the unavoidability-ratio conjecture, distinct from Problem 6 — has no Prop-node in the correspondence registry, so no statement id is grounded here.)

conjecture_5_10_at_345.title: AACL Conjecture 5.10 — proved at k ∈ {3,4,5}
conjecture_5_10_at_345.decoded: For every k with 3 ≤ k ≤ 5 and every bound N, there exists a tournament T that is k-ω̄-critical (its directed clique number ω̄(T) = k and deleting any single vertex drops ω̄ to k−1) and has more than N vertices. Since N is arbitrary, this gives arbitrarily large — hence infinitely many — k-ω̄-critical tournaments for each k in {3,4,5}, establishing Conjecture 5.10 in that range.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (2023)

conjecture_5_10_at_k3.title: AACL Conjecture 5.10 — proved at k = 3
conjecture_5_10_at_k3.decoded: For every bound N there exists a 3-ω̄-critical tournament T (directed clique number ω̄(T) = 3, with ω̄ dropping to 2 after deleting any single vertex) on more than N vertices. As N is arbitrary this yields arbitrarily large — thus infinitely many — 3-ω̄-critical tournaments, i.e. the k = 3 case of Conjecture 5.10.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (2023)

conjecture_5_10_at_k4.title: AACL Conjecture 5.10 — proved at k = 4
conjecture_5_10_at_k4.decoded: For every bound N there exists a tournament T that is 4-ω̄-critical and has more than N vertices; hence there are arbitrarily large (infinitely many) 4-ω̄-critical tournaments. This is the k = 4 instance of Conjecture 5.10 (the witnesses being the family ACₙ[C₃]).

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes

conjecture_5_10_at_k5.title: AACL Conj 5.10 — proved at k = 5
conjecture_5_10_at_k5.decoded: For every N there exists a tournament T that is 5-ω̄-critical (ω̄(T) = 5 and ω̄(T − v) = 4 for every vertex v) and has more than N vertices. This is the k = 5 instance of Conjecture 5.10: there are arbitrarily large (hence infinitely many) 5-ω̄-critical tournaments.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, 2023)

conjecture_5_10_statement.title: AACL Conjecture 5.10 — infinitely many k-ω̄-critical tournaments
conjecture_5_10_statement.decoded: For every k ≥ 3 and every bound N, there is a tournament T that is k-ω̄-critical (kcritical k T) and has more than N vertices (N < #|T|). Because the size bound N is universally quantified, this encodes "arbitrarily large k-ω̄-critical tournaments exist", i.e. infinitely many up to isomorphism, for each fixed k ≥ 3.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (2023)

conjecture_5_8_statement.title: AACL Conjecture 5.8 — bounded ω̄-cluster from large ω̄ (f ≠ id weakening)
conjecture_5_8_statement.decoded: There exist two functions f, ℓ : ℕ → ℕ such that for every tournament T and every k, if ω̄(T) ≥ f(k) then T has a vertex subset S of size at most ℓ(k) whose induced subtournament satisfies ω̄(sub_tournament S) ≥ k. This is the relaxed form of Question 5.9 in which the threshold on ω̄ is f(k) rather than k itself.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (2023)

contains_subdigraph_C3_bigger.title: C3 is contained in a strictly larger tournament
contains_subdigraph_C3_bigger.decoded: A non-reflexive containment witness: C3 embeds via inl into the 4-vertex tournament djoin C3 K1, arc-preservingly. Grounds the contains_subdigraph relation with a genuine smaller-into-larger example (rather than the identity embedding).

contains_subdigraph_refl.title: Subdigraph containment is reflexive
contains_subdigraph_refl.decoded: Proves contains_subdigraph D D for every digraph D (the identity map is injective and arc-preserving), validating the contains_subdigraph relation that the unavoidability number unvd is defined in terms of.

cyc_arc_strong.title: Directed cycle is 1-arc-strong
cyc_arc_strong.decoded: Proves arc_strong (Cyc n) 1 for n >= 1: every nonempty proper vertex set has a boundary out-arc, so the directed cycle is arc-strongly connected. Grounds the new arc-connectivity definitions outcut / arc_strong against the textbook fact lambda(directed cycle) >= 1.

dcycle_not_acyclic.title: Directed n-cycle is not acyclic (χ⃗ ≥ 2)
dcycle_not_acyclic.decoded: Proves the directed n-cycle is not acyclic: its successor map is a permutation, so it is strongly connected and every vertex lies on a directed cycle. This is the lower-bound half of χ⃗(cycle) = 2 and grounds acyclicb rejecting a cyclic digraph.

dgiso_dtri_C3.title: The hero dtri is digraph-isomorphic to C₃
dgiso_dtri_C3.decoded: Builds an explicit digraph isomorphism from dtri (= c3sub K1 (TT 1) K1) to the directed 3-cycle C3, confirming that the hero constructed by substitution really is the directed triangle. Validates that the [hero]/[c3sub] machinery produces the intended concrete object.

dhajos_added_arc.title: The directed Hajós join adds the expected arc u→w
dhajos_added_arc.decoded: On the single-arc factors, the directed Hajós join dhajos puts the arc U --> W (the new u→w arc of Def 1.5), validating that the join construction adds exactly the advertised arc.

dhajos_deleted_arc.title: The directed Hajós join deletes the original arc u→v₁
dhajos_deleted_arc.decoded: On the single-arc factors, proves ~~ (U --> M): the original arc u→v₁ is absent in the Hajós join, validating the deletion half of Def 1.5 (complementing dhajos_added_arc).

dichromatic_bounded_acyclic.title: The acyclic class is χ⃗-bounded (wrapper satisfiable)
dichromatic_bounded_acyclic.decoded: Proves the class of acyclic digraphs is dichromatic_bounded by the constant 1, exhibiting a concrete inhabitant of the dichromatic_bounded wrapper. This shows the χ⃗-bounded predicate is satisfiable and not vacuous.

dicolorableb1.title: χ⃗ ≤ 1 iff acyclic: dicolorableb D 1 = acyclicb D
dicolorableb1.decoded: Proves that a digraph is 1-dicolourable exactly when it is acyclic, anchoring the base of the Neumann-Lara dichromatic hierarchy. This validates that the boolean dicolorableb encodes χ⃗ faithfully at its smallest value.

dicolorableb2_two_sided.title: 2-dicolourability is two-sided (discriminating probe)
dicolorableb2_two_sided.decoded: Proves [dicolorableb _ 2] is TRUE on Empty2 but FALSE on a single looped vertex (Loop1), so the conclusion of conj_6_2 / thm_6_1 is genuinely discriminating, not vacuously always-true. A red-flag guard that the conjectures retain open content.

dicolorableb_C3_2.title: Directed triangle C₃ is 2-dicolourable (χ⃗(C₃) ≤ 2)
dicolorableb_C3_2.decoded: Exhibits a concrete 2-colouring of C₃ where each colour class induces an oriented digraph on at most 2 vertices, hence acyclic. Together with not_dicolorableb_C3_1 this pins χ⃗(C₃) = 2, the smallest nontrivial Neumann-Lara value.

dicolorableb_Empty2_2.title: χ⃗(Empty2) ≤ 2 — the conjectured dichromatic value
dicolorableb_Empty2_2.decoded: Proves the edgeless digraph Empty2 is 2-dicolourable (all-zero colouring induces an acyclic class). Grounds the 'value 2' conclusion of the C₃-free / S₂⁺-free / →K₂+K₁-free dichromatic conjectures (conj_6_2 / thm_6_1) on a concrete class member.

dicolorableb_monoS.title: Dicolourability is monotone in the number of colours
dicolorableb_monoS.decoded: Proves dicolorableb D k implies dicolorableb D k+1 by widening the colour codomain (the new colour class is empty hence acyclic). This validates that a faithful χ⃗-bound predicate is upward closed in k.

dicyc3_digonfree.title: The directed rim di_cycle 3 is digon-free
dicyc3_digonfree.decoded: Proves the smallest directed rim di_cycle 3 has no digon (never u-->v and v-->u together). A red-flag check that the rim is a single directed cycle, not a bidirected one; dicyc3_Eulerian additionally grounds in = out = 1.

dom_omega_cluster_statement.title: AACL Conjecture 5.3 — large domination forces a bounded ω̄-cluster
dom_omega_cluster_statement.decoded: There exist two functions f, ℓ : ℕ → ℕ such that for every tournament T and every k, if the domination number dom(T) ≥ f(k), then T has a vertex subset S of size at most ℓ(k) whose induced subtournament satisfies ω̄(sub_tournament S) ≥ k. Same bounded-witness conclusion as Conjecture 5.8 but with a domination-number hypothesis in place of the ω̄ hypothesis.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (2023)

domnum_le_omegabar.title: AACL Property 3.2 — dom(T) ≤ ω̄(T)
domnum_le_omegabar.decoded: For a tournament T, the domination number domnum (the least size of a set X such that every vertex outside X is beaten by some member of X) is at most ω̄(T), the tournament clique number. This is the lower-bound engine for ω̄ (Property 3.2 of Aboulker–Aubian–Charbit–Lopes).

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (2023)

ec_log_c6_statement.title: EC-log Eulerian SAD milestone (open conjecture) — explicit constant C = 6
ec_log_c6_statement.decoded: For every digraph D on at least 3 vertices that is Eulerian (in-degree = out-degree at every vertex), if D is k-arc-strong and the real inequality k ≥ 6·log₂(#|D|) holds (log₂ x = ln x / ln 2), then D admits a Strong Arc Decomposition. The concrete C = 6, n₀ = 3 instance of the Bang-Jensen–Yeo Eulerian logarithmic threshold; it implies the ∃C form.
ec_log_c6_statement.informal: Every Eulerian digraph on at least 3 vertices whose arc-strength k satisfies k ≥ 6·log₂ n admits a Strong Arc Decomposition (the explicit C = 6 form of the Eulerian logarithmic threshold).

ec_log_statement.title: EC-log Eulerian SAD milestone — there is an absolute constant C
ec_log_statement.decoded: There is a positive real constant c such that for every digraph D on at least 3 vertices that is Eulerian (in-degree = out-degree at every vertex), if D is k-arc-strong and k ≥ c·log₂(#|D|) (the comparison is over the reals, log₂ x = ln x / ln 2), then D admits a Strong Arc Decomposition. A logarithmic-arc-strength threshold guaranteeing SAD in the Eulerian regime of Bang-Jensen–Yeo, guarded by 3 ≤ #|D|.
ec_log_statement.informal: There is an absolute positive constant C such that every Eulerian digraph on at least 3 vertices whose arc-strength k satisfies k ≥ C·log₂ n admits a Strong Arc Decomposition.

edg_oriented.title: The edgeless digraph of every order is oriented
edg_oriented.decoded: Proves the edgeless digraph Edg n is oriented for every order n; combined with edge-freeness it is the witness of every order that made the weakened core conjectures (tvec_core / avec_core) trivially provable, motivating their fix.

edg_tfree.title: The edgeless digraph of every order is underlying-triangle-free
edg_tfree.decoded: Proves the edgeless digraph Edg n is underlying-triangle-free for every n, supplying the oriented + triangle-free witness used in the falsification sweep that exposed the trivially-provable 'core' encodings (RF1/RF2) of the t⃗(n)/a⃗(n) growth conjectures.

edges_sources_inhabited_targets_two_side.title: All implication-edge sources inhabited and targets two-sided
edges_sources_inhabited_targets_two_side.decoded: Packages the verdict of the edge sweep into one machine-checked record: every edge's source class is inhabited (CH out-degree, arc-strong C₃, C₃ dicycle, has_LFO TT) and every target is discriminating (strong/¬strong, dicycle/no dicycle, Δ*≤2 realised), so no edge is trivial or vacuous.

env_a_pos.title: Acyclic-number envelope is strictly positive for n ≥ 2
env_a_pos.decoded: Proves that the growth envelope env_a n = √(n·ln n) is strictly positive for all n ≥ 2. A Θ-tight bound on a positive combinatorial quantity (the acyclic number a⃗) must itself be positive, so this is the minimal sanity check on the env_a envelope of Conjecture 3.

env_t_pos.title: Dichromatic envelope is strictly positive for n ≥ 2
env_t_pos.decoded: Proves that the growth envelope env_t n = √(n/ln n) is strictly positive for all n ≥ 2. Sanity-checks that the env_t envelope of Conjecture 4 is a positive bound, as required for it to be a Θ-tight bound on the positive quantity t⃗.

erdos_posa_empty_ok.title: Erdos-Posa for long dicycles is not refutable on the empty digraph
erdos_posa_empty_ok.decoded: Shows the empty transversal T = set0 meets every long dicycle vacuously on TT 0, satisfying the bounded-transversal disjunct of erdos_posa_long_dicycles_statement. Confirms the statement holds on the empty digraph (no counterexample).

erdos_posa_long_dicycles_statement.title: Erdős–Pósa property for long directed cycles
erdos_posa_long_dicycles_statement.decoded: For every length threshold ℓ ≥ 2 and every n there is a bound t (a number depending on ℓ and n but not on the digraph) such that for every digraph D, either D contains n pairwise vertex-disjoint directed cycles each of length at least ℓ (a vertex-disjoint cycle pack of size n with every member of length ≥ ℓ), or there is a set T of at most t vertices that meets every directed cycle of length ≥ ℓ (meets_long_dicycles ℓ T).
erdos_posa_long_dicycles_statement.match_title: Erdős-Pósa property for long directed cycles

gdhajos_v2w_deleted.title: The directed Hajós join deletes the second original arc v₂w
gdhajos_v2w_deleted.decoded: Proves that in the directed Hajós join dhajos, the original arc v₂→w of the second factor is gone between the merged vertex and w (M --> W is false). Grounds the second arc-deletion clause of the Def-1.5 Hajós-join encoding, completing the added-arc/deleted-arc faithfulness check.

gr_C3_card_gt0.title: C₃ is nonempty (0 < #|C₃|)
gr_C3_card_gt0.decoded: The directed 3-cycle C₃ has at least one vertex (0 < #|C₃|), discharging the nonemptiness guard used by the classic-core and Cheng–Keevash statements on a concrete witness.

gr_C3_minoutdeg1.title: C₃ has minimum out-degree ≥ 1
gr_C3_minoutdeg1.decoded: Every vertex of C₃ has out-degree at least 1, so C₃ meets the minimum-out-degree hypothesis (δ⁺ ≥ 1) of the long-path / cycle statements at the smallest nontrivial value.

gr_C3_outdeg.title: Every vertex of C₃ has out-degree 1
gr_C3_outdeg.decoded: In the directed 3-cycle C₃ every vertex has out-degree exactly 1, exercising the outdeg invariant on a concrete oriented graph (the δ⁺ = 1 regime of Caccetta–Häggkvist and Cheng–Keevash).

gr_Delta_star_le_id.title: Degreewidth is bounded by the identity order's back-degree
gr_Delta_star_le_id.decoded: Confirms Δ*(T) is genuinely a minimum over orders by exhibiting the identity order as an upper-bound witness: Δ*(T) ≤ maxbackdeg(1). Tests that the Delta_star definition is the min-over-orders quantity it is meant to be.

gr_Theta_2n_n.title: Θ is satisfiable with explicit constants: 2n = Θ(n)
gr_Theta_2n_n.decoded: Exhibits a concrete instance 2·n = Θ(n) with explicit witnessing constants c1 = 1, c2 = 2 and threshold N = 0. Shows is_Theta is a satisfiable, non-vacuous predicate, so the Θ-conjectures of reals_growth.v do not quantify over an empty relation.

gr_concrete_otww_refines.title: Concrete ordered twin-width refines (unordered) twin-width
gr_concrete_otww_refines.decoded: Bridges the ordered and unordered twin-width definitions: a concrete ordered-tww bound (concrete_otww_le) entails the unordered tww_le bound, since an ordered contraction sequence is a contraction sequence. Validates the twinwidth_ordered ↔ twinwidth definitions used by the bounded-twin-width tournament conjectures.

gr_di3_not_di4.title: A directed 3-cycle is not a directed 4-cycle
gr_di3_not_di4.decoded: Discriminating probe: the length-3 and length-4 directed-cycle predicates di3cycle and di4cycle are mutually exclusive, so they genuinely separate by cycle length. Validates that these refined cycle-length predicates are not collapsed.

gr_di3cycle_dicycle.title: A directed 3-cycle is a directed cycle
gr_di3cycle_dicycle.decoded: Confirms di3cycle is a genuine refinement of dicycle (every length-3 directed cycle is a directed cycle), validating that the di3cycle predicate strengthens rather than diverges from the base dicycle definition.

gr_ell_C3.title: Longest directed path of C₃ has length 2 (ℓ(C₃) = 2)
gr_ell_C3.decoded: The longest-directed-simple-path invariant ℓ equals 2 on the directed 3-cycle. This grounds the ℓ used in Cheng–Keevash and shows the bound ℓ ≥ 2δ is tight at δ = 1 (ℓ(C₃) = 2 = 2·1).

gr_eulerian_TT1.title: The one-vertex digraph is Eulerian (eulerian non-vacuous)
gr_eulerian_TT1.decoded: Proves that the single-vertex transitive tournament TT 1 is Eulerian (in-degree = out-degree = 0 at its only vertex). Inhabits the antecedent class of the EC-log / SAD milestone, confirming the eulerian hypothesis of ec_log_statement is non-vacuous.

gr_has_LFO_Delta_le2.title: has_LFO implies degreewidth ≤ 2
gr_has_LFO_Delta_le2.decoded: Re-exposes the proved Path-FAS reduction as a clean edge: any tournament with a linear-forest ordering has degreewidth at most 2 (a linear-forest order has max back-degree ≤ 2). Links the has_LFO and Delta_star definitions of the two FAS conjectures.

gr_log2_2.title: log₂ 2 = 1 (independent re-grounding of the base-2 log)
gr_log2_2.decoded: Independently re-proves log2 2 = 1 in the deeper grounding pass, confirming reals_growth.v's log2 is the genuine base-2 logarithm used in the EC-log SAD threshold.

grounding_hero_dtri.title: The directed triangle is a Berger hero
grounding_hero_dtri.decoded: Proves the directed triangle (the substitution dtri = c3sub K1 (TT 1) K1) satisfies the inductive predicate [hero] via one [hero_c3L] step. Exhibits the simplest non-transitive hero, so [hero] is a satisfiable predicate beyond the base vertex.

grounding_transitive_TT_is_hero.title: Every nonempty transitive tournament is a hero (iso-closure)
grounding_transitive_TT_is_hero.decoded: Re-derives Berger's fact that every nonempty transitive tournament TT n.+1 is a hero, in the iso-closure sense [is_hero] (since the inductive [hero] only yields djoin/c3sub vertex types), via the key isomorphism TT l.+1 ≅ djoin K1 (TT l). Grounds that transitive tournaments populate the hero class.

gw3_is_wheel.title: The smallest generalised wheel W₃ has the full advertised structure
gw3_is_wheel.decoded: Instantiates the committed structural package at the smallest generalised wheel gwheel 3: Eulerian, digon graph a forest, underlying graph 2-connected, and a member of the concretely-generated H₂. Grounds that the smallest H₂ tree-join family member is a faithful wheel.

gwheel3_in_H2.title: The wheel W₃ inhabits the concretely-generated H₂
gwheel3_in_H2.decoded: Proves in_H2_concrete realises_gw (gwheel 3): the generalised wheel W₃ is a member of the concretely-generated family H₂ via the star datum, so the tree-join membership predicate is non-vacuous with a real digraph witness.

has_LFO_C3.title: The directed triangle has a linear-forest ordering
has_LFO_C3.decoded: Exhibits a concrete linear-forest ordering of the directed triangle C₃ (the identity order, whose back-arc graph is the single edge {0,2}), so has_LFO is a satisfiable predicate even on a non-transitive tournament. Validates the linear-forest-ordering definition that the Path-FAS = LFO reduction quantifies over.

has_LFO_TT.title: Every transitive tournament has a linear-forest ordering
has_LFO_TT.decoded: Shows has_LFO(TTₙ) holds for every n: the identity order of a transitive tournament has an edgeless (hence linear-forest) back-arc graph. The transitive non-vacuity witness for the has_LFO predicate of the Path-FAS = LFO reduction.

has_LFO_edge_on_TT.title: Δ*(TT n) ≤ 2 — the has-LFO edge realised on TT n
has_LFO_edge_on_TT.decoded: Applies the committed has_LFO ⇒ Δ* ≤ 2 edge to the witness TT n, computing Δ*(TT n) ≤ 2; confirms the edge's conclusion is reachable on a concrete tournament.

hoang_reed_antecedent_C3.title: Hoang-Reed antecedent is inhabited at k = 1
hoang_reed_antecedent_C3.decoded: Shows C3 is nonempty and every vertex has out-degree >= 1, so the now-guarded Hoang-Reed antecedent is satisfied by C3. Confirms the guard fix yields a non-empty hypothesis class.

hoang_reed_k1_holds.title: Hoang-Reed holds at k = 1 on nonempty digraphs
hoang_reed_k1_holds.decoded: The k = 1 slice of Hoang-Reed is a theorem: every nonempty digraph of min out-degree >= 1 has a cycle pack of size 1 of dicycles. Confirms the (guarded) hoang_reed_statement is correctly calibrated at k = 1, where it must be true and is.

hoang_reed_statement.title: Hoàng–Reed Conjecture
hoang_reed_statement.decoded: For every nonempty digraph D (0 < #|D|) and every k, if every vertex has out-degree at least k (pointwise, forall v: k ≤ outdeg v), then D contains a pack P of k directed cycles (cycle_pack P, size P = k) with the intersection-forest property: for each position j > 0, the set of vertices on cycle j that also lie on some earlier cycle i < j has size at most one — i.e. each cycle meets the union of all the previous ones in at most one vertex.

in_H2_concrete_inhabited.title: The H₂ class is inhabited by the symmetric triangle (any realisation)
in_H2_concrete_inhabited.decoded: Proves the symmetric triangle sym_cycle 3 is a base member of [in_H2_concrete] for any realisation relation, so the H₂ side of the conj_9_2_concrete biconditional is non-vacuous and the statement is well-formed on both sides.

is_Theta_const_mul.title: Constant factors are absorbed by Θ: 2·f = Θ(f)
is_Theta_const_mul.decoded: Proves that doubling a sequence leaves its Θ-class unchanged (2·f = Θ(f)). Validates the textbook law that constant factors do not matter for Θ, confirming is_Theta is non-degenerate and behaves as required by the Θ-conjectures.

is_Theta_refl.title: Θ is reflexive: f = Θ(f)
is_Theta_refl.decoded: Proves that every real sequence f is Θ(f) (with constants c1 = c2 = 1). Reflexivity is one of the three textbook properties of an asymptotically-tight Landau bound, so this validates that reals_growth.v's is_Theta really encodes a tight-bound relation underlying the Θ-conjectures.

is_Theta_sym.title: Θ is symmetric: f = Θ(g) ⟹ g = Θ(f)
is_Theta_sym.decoded: Proves that is_Theta is symmetric, inverting the two bounding constants (c1, c2) ↦ (1/c2, 1/c1). Symmetry is the red-flag property: if it failed, is_Theta would not encode a two-sided tight bound and the Θ-conjectures would be mis-stated.

is_Theta_trans.title: Θ is transitive: f = Θ(g), g = Θ(h) ⟹ f = Θ(h)
is_Theta_trans.decoded: Proves that is_Theta composes (via the Big-O / Big-Omega preorder split), completing the verification that reals_growth.v's Θ is a genuine equivalence relation rather than an arbitrary predicate.

is_tournament_TT.title: Every transitive tournament TT n satisfies the spelled-out is_tournament predicate
is_tournament_TT.decoded: Shows the hand-written predicate [is_tournament] (irreflexive + semicomplete + asymmetric) holds for every TT n. A definitional sanity check that the spelled-out predicate agrees with the [tournament] HB structure on the canonical instances (and likewise is_tournament_C3 for C₃).

jackson_hamilton_small_diregular_stateme.title: Jackson's Conjecture — Hamilton cycle in small d-diregular oriented graphs
jackson_hamilton_small_diregular_stateme.decoded: For every nonempty oriented (digon-free) digraph D (0 < #|D|) and every d > 2 (i.e. d ≥ 3) such that D is d-diregular (diregular D d, i.e. every vertex has in-degree EXACTLY d and out-degree EXACTLY d — not merely ≥ d) and D has at most 4d+1 vertices, there is a directed cycle whose size equals #|D|, i.e. a Hamilton directed cycle through every vertex.
jackson_hamilton_small_diregular_stateme.match_title: Hamilton cycle in small d-diregular graphs

kcritical2_uniq.title: Uniqueness of the 2-ω̄-critical tournament (C₃)
kcritical2_uniq.decoded: Every 2-ω̄-critical tournament T (i.e. T satisfying kcritical 2 T: ω̄(T) = 2 and deleting any single vertex drops ω̄ to 1) is digraph-isomorphic to C₃, the directed triangle. Hence C₃ is the unique 2-ω̄-critical tournament.
kcritical2_uniq.informal: Up to digraph isomorphism the only 2-ω̄-critical tournament is the directed 3-cycle C₃.

kcritical_proper_sub.title: Proper subtournaments of a k-ω̄-critical tournament have ω̄ ≤ k−1
kcritical_proper_sub.decoded: For every natural number k and every k-ω̄-critical tournament T, any vertex subset S that is NOT the whole vertex set (S ≠ [set: T], a proper subset) induces a subtournament with ω̄(sub_tournament S) ≤ k−1. A proper subset omits some vertex, so it embeds into that vertex's deletion, which has ω̄ = k−1 by criticality.
kcritical_proper_sub.informal: In a k-ω̄-critical tournament every proper subtournament has directed clique number ω̄ ≤ k − 1.

kmajority_col_TT2.title: TT 2 admits a (1/k)-fractional majority colouring for every k
kmajority_col_TT2.decoded: The identity 2-colouring of TT 2 makes every same-colour out-neighbourhood empty, so kmajority_col k holds for every k, validating the parametric (1/k)·deg⁺ head of the fractional majority colouring conjecture (Conj 9) as a satisfiable predicate.

kmajority_k1_head_TT2.title: Conj 9 existential head at k = 2 holds on TT 2 with 3 colours
kmajority_k1_head_TT2.decoded: Exhibits a 3-colouring of TT 2 satisfying kmajority_col 2 (the k+1 = 3 colour, (1/2)·deg⁺ bound), confirming the existential head of the fractional majority colouring conjecture is realisable at k = 2 on a concrete digraph.

lambda_Cyc.title: Arc-connectivity of the directed cycle equals 1
lambda_Cyc.decoded: Computes lambda (Cyc n) = 1 for n >= 2 by combining the >= 1 bound with a singleton out-cut of size exactly 1. A key value computation grounding the lambda big-min definition: the minimum out-cut of a directed cycle is exactly one arc.

lemma7.title: Lemma 7 (Cheng–Keevash) — dichotomy: long path or a deficient set
lemma7.decoded: For every nonempty oriented graph D (0 < #|D|, no loops, no digons) and every δ with every vertex of out-degree at least δ, EITHER the longest directed simple path has length at least 2δ (ℓ(D) ≥ 2δ — the full conjecture conclusion), OR there exists a nonempty vertex set S (S ≠ ∅) of size at most δ (#|S| ≤ δ) such that every vertex v in S has at least 2δ − ℓ(D) of its out-neighbours inside S (outdeg_in S v ≥ 2δ − ℓ(D), nat truncated subtraction). The general structural lemma whose two branches drive the δ = 2 and δ = 3 results.
lemma7.match_title: Directed path of length twice the minimum outdegree

linial_berge_empty_ok.title: Linial-Berge is not refutable on the empty digraph
linial_berge_empty_ok.decoded: Exhibits the empty path-partition and S = set0 as witnesses satisfying every clause of linial_berge_statement on TT 0 for each colour bound k >= 1, so it holds there. Confirms Linial-Berge has no empty-digraph counterexample.

linial_berge_statement.title: Linial–Berge path-partition duality
linial_berge_statement.decoded: For every digraph D and every k there exist a path-partition Q of V(D) (Q partitions every vertex into vertex-disjoint directed paths, pp_partition Q) and a vertex set S inducing a subdigraph with directed chromatic number ≤ k (dicolorableb (D[S]) k, i.e. χ⃗(D[S]) ≤ k) such that the k-norm of Q — the sum over paths of min(k, number of vertices on the path) — equals |S| (pp_knorm k Q = #|S|), and this is optimal: no path-partition Q' has k-norm smaller than |S| (forall Q' path-partition, #|S| ≤ pp_knorm k Q'). In other words the minimum k-norm over path-partitions equals the maximum order of an induced χ⃗ ≤ k subdigraph.

log2_2.title: log₂ 2 = 1 (base-2 logarithm sanity)
log2_2.decoded: Proves that log2 2 = 1, the defining value of a base-2 logarithm. Grounds the log2 (= ln x / ln 2) used in the EC-log SAD threshold k ≥ c·log₂ n, confirming log2 really is the base-2 logarithm.

long_dicycle_diregular_statement.title: Long directed cycles in diregular oriented graphs (Bermond–Germa–Heydemann–Sotteau)
long_dicycle_diregular_statement.decoded: For every nonempty oriented (digon-free) digraph D (0 < #|D|) and every d ≥ 1 such that D is strongly connected and every vertex has in-degree at least d and out-degree at least d, there is a directed cycle of length at least 2d+1.
long_dicycle_diregular_statement.match_title: Long directed cycles in diregular digraphs

loop_not_dicolorable.title: A loop kills dicolourability at every k
loop_not_dicolorable.decoded: Proves that if any vertex carries a loop then D is not k-dicolourable for any k, since the loop is a length-1 dicycle inside its own colour class. This discriminating probe confirms dicolorableb correctly rejects degenerate (non-acyclic) inputs.

m3_edge_is_theorem.title: m(3) landmark implies the triangle-free class is not 2-χ⃗-bounded
m3_edge_is_theorem.decoded: Re-exposes the clean implication that IF the m(3) witness (a triangle-free oriented graph with χ⃗ ≥ 3) exists THEN the oriented triangle-free class is not 2-dicolourable-bounded. This confirms the m(3) edge is a genuine Qed implication, while the open m3_landmark_statement itself stays correctly unproven.

m3_landmark_statement.title: The m(3) landmark — a triangle-free oriented graph with dichromatic number ≥ 3
m3_landmark_statement.decoded: There exists a nonempty (0 < #|D|), digon-free digraph D whose underlying graph is triangle-free and that is NOT 2-dicolourable (its vertices cannot be split into 2 acyclic-inducing classes), so its dichromatic number is at least 3. This witnesses that the least order m(3) realizing dichromatic number 3 among oriented triangle-free graphs is finite.
