source.attribution: Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al.

mad_C3_eq2.title: Maximum average degree of C3 equals 2
mad_C3_eq2.decoded: Computes mad C3 = 2 (3 vertices, 3 arcs, density 2; no denser subset). A key value computation grounding the mad / density / narcs_in definitions used in Problem 6's mad-bounded hypothesis.

majority_3col_C3.title: Directed triangle C3 has a majority 3-colouring
majority_3col_C3.decoded: Exhibits a concrete majority 3-colouring of C3 (the rainbow identity colouring, where every vertex's unique out-neighbour gets a different colour), validating the existential head of the majority 3-colouring conjecture on a small instance and confirming majority_col is satisfiable.

majority_3col_TT2.title: The directed path TT 2 admits a majority 3-colouring
majority_3col_TT2.decoded: Exhibits a 3-colouring of the single arc TT 2 whose same-colour out-neighbourhood is empty everywhere, so the existential head of the majority 3-colouring conjecture holds on the path. This grounds majority_3col as satisfiable on a concrete small digraph.

majority_3col_eulerian_statement.title: Majority 3-colouring of Eulerian digraphs (KOSvZW, Open Problem 3)
majority_3col_eulerian_statement.decoded: Every Eulerian digraph D (in-degree equals out-degree at every vertex) admits a majority 3-colouring: a 3-colouring of its vertices such that at every vertex v at most half of its out-neighbours share v's own colour (2·#{w : v→w, col w = col v} ≤ outdeg v for all v). The Eulerian special case of the majority 3-colouring conjecture.
majority_3col_eulerian_statement.match_title: Open Problem 3 (1608.03040): Does every Eulerian digraph have a majority 3-colouring?

source.name: arXiv 1608.03040
source.attribution: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen, Wood (2017)

majority_3col_statement.title: Majority 3-colouring Conjecture (Kreutzer–Oum–Seymour–van der Zypen–Wood, Conj 2)
majority_3col_statement.decoded: Every digraph D admits a vertex colouring with 3 colours that is a majority colouring: at every vertex v at most half of its out-neighbours share v's own colour (encoded fraction-free as 2·#{w : v→w, col w = col v} ≤ outdeg v for all v).
majority_3col_statement.match_title: Conjecture 2 (1608.03040): Every digraph has a majority 3-colouring

source.name: arXiv 1608.03040
source.attribution: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen, Wood (2017)

majority_3col_tournament_statement.title: Majority 3-colouring of tournaments (KOSvZW, Open Problem 2)
majority_3col_tournament_statement.decoded: Every tournament T admits a majority 3-colouring: a colouring of its vertices with 3 colours such that at every vertex v at most half of its out-neighbours share v's own colour (2·#{w : v→w, col w = col v} ≤ outdeg v for all v). The tournament special case of the majority 3-colouring conjecture.
majority_3col_tournament_statement.match_title: Open Problem 2 (1608.03040): Does every tournament have a majority 3-colouring?

source.name: arXiv 1608.03040
source.attribution: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen, Wood (2017)

majority_col_C3.title: C₃ admits a majority colouring (the proper 'Z₃ colouring)
majority_col_C3.decoded: Proves the proper colouring of C₃ (each vertex its own colour) is a majority colouring, since no vertex shares its colour with its out-neighbour so the same-colour out-neighbourhood is empty. This grounds majority_col as satisfiable, validating the existential head of the majority-colouring conjecture.

majority_k1col_statement.title: Fractional majority colouring (Kreutzer–Oum–Seymour–van der Zypen–Wood, Conj 9)
majority_k1col_statement.decoded: For every k ≥ 2, every digraph D admits a vertex colouring with k+1 colours such that at every vertex v at most a (1/k)-fraction of its out-neighbours share v's colour (encoded fraction-free as k·#{w : v→w, col w = col v} ≤ outdeg v for all v). At k = 2 this is exactly the majority 3-colouring condition.
majority_k1col_statement.match_title: Conjecture 9 (1608.03040): (k+1)-colouring with at most (1/k)deg^+ same-coloured out-neighbours

source.name: arXiv 1608.03040
source.attribution: Kreutzer, Oum, Seymour, van der Zypen, Wood (2017)

matchingFAS_iff_dw1_statement.title: Matching-FAS = degreewidth at most 1 (sparse-tournament fact)
matchingFAS_iff_dw1_statement.decoded: For every tournament T, T admits a matching feedback arc set if and only if its degreewidth Δ*(T) is at most 1. A matching-FAS is an arc set meeting every directed cycle whose underlying simple graph is a matching (a forest of maximum degree at most 1: no two of its arcs share a vertex). Δ*(T) is the minimum over all vertex orders of the maximum back-degree of a vertex (realized as a genuine minimum). Stated as a Prop target — a known result of Davot–Isenmann–Roy–Thiebaut stated uniformly here, not proved.

source.name: arXiv 2212.06007
source.attribution: Davot, Isenmann, Roy, Thiebaut

merge_step_C3.title: The contraction machinery can take an honest merge step on C₃
merge_step_C3.decoded: Discriminating probe: on the 3-vertex graph C3 the discrete partition admits a real merge_step fusing two inequivalent vertices, witnessing that the contraction trigraph machinery underlying tww_le is non-degenerate (not stuck at the singleton sequence). The contraction machinery underlies tww_le, which appears only in Conjecture 3.12.

min_outdeg_dicycle.title: Min out-degree >= 1 forces a directed cycle (packing base case)
min_outdeg_dicycle.decoded: Proves that any finite nonempty digraph in which every vertex has out-degree >= 1 contains a dicycle (follow out-arcs until a vertex repeats). This is the k = 1 base case of Hoang-Reed / Bermond-Thomassen, established unconditionally under the explicit 0 < #|D| guard (the same guard the two statements now carry to exclude the empty-digraph counterexample).

minimal_LFO_no_infinite_statement.title: Infinitely many vertex-minimal LFO obstructions
minimal_LFO_no_infinite_statement.decoded: There are arbitrarily large vertex-minimal LFO-obstructions: for every N there is a tournament T on more than N vertices (N < #|T|) that has no linear-forest ordering, yet is vertex-minimal — deleting ANY single vertex yields a tournament that DOES have a linear-forest ordering. So the family of vertex-minimal LFO-NO tournaments is infinite, which (via the conjectured Path-FAS ⇔ LFO reduction) would rule out any finite forbidden-subtournament characterization of Path-FAS.

source.name: arXiv 2402.10782
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Lopes

mono_reach_or_rainbow_class_inhabited.title: Non-vacuity: a nonempty 3-arc-coloured tournament exists
mono_reach_or_rainbow_class_inhabited.decoded: Exhibits the directed triangle C3 as a nonempty tournament carrying a 3-arc-colouring, so the antecedent class of mono_reach_or_rainbow_statement is inhabited and the universal quantification is not vacuously about an empty class.

mono_reach_or_rainbow_statement.title: Monochromatic reachability or rainbow triangles (Sands–Sauer–Woodrow variant)
mono_reach_or_rainbow_statement.decoded: For every nonempty tournament T (0 < #|T|) whose arcs are coloured with 3 colours, either T contains a rainbow directed triangle (a directed 3-cycle a→b→c→a whose three arcs carry three distinct colours) or T has a monochromatic-reachability root: a vertex v from which every vertex w is reachable by a single-colour directed path (the colour may depend on w).

mono_root_tt2_0.title: TT 2 has a monochromatic-reachability root
mono_root_tt2_0.decoded: On the 1-arc-coloured TT 2, vertex 0 reaches every vertex in a single colour (itself reflexively, vertex 1 via the coloured arc), so mono_root is a satisfiable predicate and the right disjunct of mono_reach_or_rainbow_statement is realisable.

no_rainbow_c3const3.title: Refutation probe: constant-coloured C3 has no rainbow triangle
no_rainbow_c3const3.decoded: The constant 3-arc-colouring of C3 has NO rainbow triangle (all three arcs share a colour), so rainbow_triangle is genuinely falsifiable; the rainbow disjunct of mono_reach_or_rainbow_statement is not provable for free.

no_short_strong3.title: Cheng–Keevash δ = 3 endgame — no short strong 3-out-regular oriented digraph
no_short_strong3.decoded: It is NOT the case that a nonempty (0 < #|H|), strongly connected (strongb H), oriented digraph H (no loops, no digons) in which every vertex has out-degree EXACTLY 3 has its longest directed simple path of length at most 5: such an H with ℓ(H) ≤ 5 yields a contradiction (False). The internal endgame lemma forcing ℓ(H) ≥ 6 in the strong 3-out-regular case that underlies the δ = 3 theorem.
no_short_strong3.informal: No nonempty strongly connected oriented digraph in which every vertex has out-degree exactly 3 has its longest directed simple path of length ≤ 5; equivalently, every such digraph has a directed path of length ≥ 6.

not_arc_strong_C3_2.title: Directed triangle C3 is not 2-arc-strong
not_arc_strong_C3_2.decoded: A discriminating probe: C3 is NOT 2-arc-strong because the singleton {0} has out-cut exactly the one arc 0 -> 1, of size 1 < 2. Pins the minimum out-cut of C3 to 1 and confirms arc_strong is a genuine sliding threshold, not trivially satisfied at higher k.

not_dicolorableb_C3_1.title: Directed triangle C₃ is not 1-dicolourable (χ⃗(C₃) > 1)
not_dicolorableb_C3_1.decoded: Red-flag probe: proves C₃ is NOT 1-dicolourable (its negation is the theorem), so χ⃗(C₃) > 1. If dicolorableb C3 1 were provable the dichromatic encoding would be broken; this confirms the directed triangle is the smallest non-acyclic example.

not_kmajority_col2_c3const2.title: Refutation probe: constant-coloured C3 fails majority (kmajority_col 2)
not_kmajority_col2_c3const2.decoded: The constant 2-colouring of C3 violates kmajority_col 2 (each vertex's unique out-neighbour shares its colour: 2·1 > 1 = outdeg), so the majority condition is not vacuously satisfied by every colouring and majority_3col_statement is non-trivial.

not_majority_col_c3const.title: Constant colouring of C₃ is not a majority colouring
not_majority_col_c3const.decoded: Red-flag probe: the constant colouring of C₃ fails majority_col, because each vertex's single out-neighbour shares its colour (2·1 = 2 > 1 = outdeg). This shows majority_col genuinely constrains colourings and is not vacuously true, so the existential in the majority-colouring conjecture has real content.

not_mono_reach_tt2_col0.title: Refutation probe: no monochromatic root when the colour is absent
not_mono_reach_tt2_col0.decoded: On a TT 2 whose only arc carries colour 1, vertex 0 does NOT reach vertex 1 in colour 0 (the colour-0 sub-relation is empty), so mono_root genuinely depends on the colouring and the disjunction in mono_reach_or_rainbow_statement has real content.

not_unvd_K1_0.title: Red-flag probe: unvd(K₁) ≠ 0
not_unvd_K1_0.decoded: Refutes the degenerate value unvd(K₁) = 0: the empty tournament TT 0 admits no injection from K₁, so 0 is not unavoidable. A discriminating probe ensuring the 'least unavoidable N' framing of unvd is not vacuously satisfiable at 0.

ochi_le_TT2.title: A single arc is 2-oriented-colourable (ochi ≤ 2)
ochi_le_TT2.decoded: Proves the single arc TT 2 maps homomorphically onto the 2-vertex tournament, so ochi_le (TT 2) 2 holds. This is the smallest non-vacuous value of the oriented chromatic number, grounding oriented_kcolouring / ochi_le.

odd_par_tree_fails.title: A mismatched-parity tree fails even-B-parity
odd_par_tree_fails.decoded: Refutation probe: exhibits odd_par_tree (one B-leaf, one A-then-B leaf of opposite B-parity) and proves ~~ even_B_parity, showing the Def-9.1 parity check is a genuine discriminating filter rather than an always-true predicate.

omegabar_AC.title: ω̄(ACₙ) = 3
omegabar_AC.decoded: For m ≥ 3 (ACₙ a circulant tournament on n = 2m+1 ≥ 7 vertices), the directed clique number of ACₙ equals 3.
omegabar_AC.informal: For m ≥ 3, the circulant tournament ACₙ (on n = 2m+1 vertices) has directed clique number ω̄(ACₙ) = 3.

omegabar_AC_del.title: ω̄(ACₙ − v) = 2
omegabar_AC_del.decoded: For m ≥ 3 and any vertex v of ACₙ, deleting v from the circulant tournament ACₙ yields a tournament with directed clique number ω̄ = 2.
omegabar_AC_del.informal: For m ≥ 3 and any vertex v, deleting v from the circulant tournament ACₙ yields a tournament with directed clique number ω̄ = 2.

omegabar_C3_val.title: ω̄(C₃) = 2
omegabar_C3_val.decoded: Pins the backedge clique number invariant ω̄(C3) = 2 on the directed triangle, anchoring the genuinely-computed RHS quantity that the binding-function conjectures bound (so the conjecture statements range over a real invariant, not a placeholder).

omegabar_T4.title: ω̄(T4 = ACₙ[C₃]) = 4
omegabar_T4.decoded: The tournament T4 = ACₙ[C₃] (with n = 2m+1, m ≥ 3) has ω̄(T4) = 4, where ω̄ is the AACL clique-number invariant of a tournament. This is the value pinned for the 4-ω̄-criticality argument.
omegabar_T4.informal: The tournament T4 = ACₙ[C₃] has directed clique number ω̄(T4) = 4.

omegabar_T4_del.title: ω̄(T4 − v) = 3 for every vertex
omegabar_T4_del.decoded: For every vertex v of T4 = ACₙ[C₃] (n = 2m+1, m ≥ 3), the tournament obtained by deleting v has ω̄(T4 − v) = 3. (One value suffices for all v by vertex-transitivity of T4.) This is the criticality drop feeding T4_kcritical4.
omegabar_T4_del.informal: For every vertex v, deleting v from the tournament T4 = ACₙ[C₃] yields a tournament with directed clique number ω̄ = 3.

omegabar_T5.title: ω̄(T₅) = 5
omegabar_T5.decoded: The directed clique number of the host tournament T₅ = ACₙ[ACₙ] (odd n = 2m+1, m ≥ 3) is exactly 5. This is the upper component of the criticality witness.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, 2023)

omegabar_T5_del.title: ω̄(T₅ − v) = 4 for every vertex v
omegabar_T5_del.decoded: Deleting any single vertex v from the host tournament T₅ = ACₙ[ACₙ] yields a tournament with directed clique number exactly 4. By vertex-transitivity of the product, the value at one vertex spreads to all, so every single-vertex deletion drops ω̄ from 5 to 4.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, 2023)

omegabar_del_vt.title: Vertex-deletion of ω̄ is vertex-independent on vertex-transitive tournaments
omegabar_del_vt.decoded: If a tournament T is vertex-transitive (vertex_transitiveb T), then ω̄(del_tournament u) = ω̄(del_tournament v) for all vertices u, v — deleting any vertex leaves a subtournament with the same clique number, regardless of which vertex is removed.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (2023)

omegabar_lexprod_ge.title: ω̄ of a lexicographic substitution — lower bound
omegabar_lexprod_ge.decoded: For tournaments S and H, the lexicographic substitution S[H] (vertices are pairs (s,h), with (s₁,h₁) → (s₂,h₂) iff s₁ → s₂, or s₁ = s₂ and h₁ → h₂) satisfies ω̄(S) + ω̄(H) ≤ ω̄(S[H]) + 1, i.e. ω̄(S[H]) ≥ ω̄(S) + ω̄(H) − 1. The clique number is super-additive under substitution.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (2023)

omegabar_transb.title: ω̄(T) = 1 if and only if T is transitive
omegabar_transb.decoded: For every nonempty tournament T (0 < #|T|), ω̄(T) equals 1 if and only if T is transitive (transb T, i.e. the arc relation is transitive). Equivalently, transitive tournaments are exactly the ones with clique number 1.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (2023)

oriented_chromatic_planar_bounded_statem.title: Oriented chromatic number of planar graphs is bounded (Courcelle; Sopena)
oriented_chromatic_planar_bounded_statem.decoded: There is a single number k such that every loopless digraph D whose underlying simple graph is planar (in the Wagner/minor sense: no K₅ and no K₃,₃ minor) admits an oriented k-colouring, encoded as a digraph homomorphism (arc-preserving map, not required surjective) from D to some tournament on exactly k vertices. The bound k is uniform over the whole class. Note: the formal statement quantifies over every loopless digraph with planar underlying graph (not only orientations); there is no 0 < #|D| nonemptiness guard.
oriented_chromatic_planar_bounded_statem.match_title: Oriented chromatic number of planar graphs

oriented_dg_tournament.title: Every tournament is oriented
oriented_dg_tournament.decoded: Proves the arc relation of any tournament is asymmetric, so oriented_dg holds of every tournament (and of C₃). This validates that the oriented_dg predicate is correct: its negation on C₃ is unprovable, confirming the antecedent class of the Forb(H) conjectures is faithfully captured.

oriented_forest_Empty2.title: The edgeless 2-vertex digraph is an oriented forest (antecedent non-vacuity)
oriented_forest_Empty2.decoded: Proves the underlying graph of the edgeless digraph Empty2 is a forest, giving a ≥2-vertex witness that the 'F an oriented forest / star forest' antecedent of the hero dichotomy (and its conj_4_2 RHS disjunct) is satisfiable, not an empty class.

oriented_kcolouring_C3.title: C₃ has an oriented 3-colouring (onto itself)
oriented_kcolouring_C3.decoded: Proves C₃ maps homomorphically onto a 3-vertex tournament (itself), so oriented_kcolouring C3 3 holds. This non-vacuity witness shows the oriented chromatic number predicate is inhabited and finite.

pathFAS_iff_LFO_statement.title: Path-FAS = linear-forest ordering (the LFO reduction)
pathFAS_iff_LFO_statement.decoded: For every tournament T, T has a path-shaped feedback arc set if and only if T has a linear-forest ordering. The left side (has_pathFAS) asks for a set F of genuine arcs that meets every directed cycle and whose underlying simple graph (vertices adjacent iff F has an arc between them in either direction) is a linear forest — acyclic with every vertex of degree at most 2. The right side (has_LFO) asks for a vertex order whose back-arc graph is such a linear forest. Stated as a Prop target; its proof is the open reduction.

source.name: arXiv 2402.10782
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Lopes (Problem 4.4)

planar_class_loopless_inhabited.title: Non-vacuity: a loopless oriented digraph exists
planar_class_loopless_inhabited.decoded: Exhibits TT 2 (a single arc) as a concrete loopless digraph, inhabiting the looplessness side-condition of oriented_chromatic_planar_bounded_statement's antecedent (the lemma proves only loopless; planarity of the single edge is supplied at the two_extremal level), so the antecedent class is not quantified over an empty looplessness universe.

prob6_unvd_statement.title: Problem 6 (Aboulker–Bang-Jensen et al.) — bounded-mad ⇒ polynomial unavoidability bound
prob6_unvd_statement.decoded: For every rational bound α, there exist nat coefficients a, d, b such that every nonempty digraph D (0 < #|D|) whose maximum average degree is at most α has unavoidability number nD bounded by the polynomial a·#|D|^d + b (here nD is pinned via the unvd relation: the least N such that every N-vertex tournament contains D). Faithfully encodes 'there exists a polynomial P_α with unvd(D) ≤ P_α(|V(D)|)' for bounded-mad digraphs, with the polynomial given concretely by coefficients a, d, b.

source.name: arXiv 2410.23566
source.attribution: Aboulker, Bang-Jensen, Bousquet, Charbit, Havet et al.

prob_6_class_inhabited.title: Problem 6 hypothesis class is inhabited at alpha = 2
prob_6_class_inhabited.decoded: Shows C3 is a nonempty digraph with mad C3 <= 2, so for alpha = 2 the mad-bounded family quantified by Problem 6 is non-empty. Confirms prob_6's per-alpha universal is not a statement about an empty class.

question_5_9_fails_at_k3.title: AACL Question 5.9 — refuted at k = 3
question_5_9_fails_at_k3.decoded: For every bound L there exists a 3-ω̄-critical tournament T with more than L vertices such that the only subtournament S with ω̄(sub_tournament S) ≥ 3 is the whole vertex set T itself. So T witnesses ω̄ ≥ 3 only via an arbitrarily large subtournament, refuting the k = 3 case of Question 5.9 (which asked for a bounded-size, ℓ(k)-bounded, subtournament certifying ω̄ ≥ k).

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (2023)

question_5_9_fails_at_k4.title: AACL Question 5.9 fails at k = 4
question_5_9_fails_at_k4.decoded: For every bound L there is a tournament T that is 4-ω̄-critical, has more than L vertices, and in which the ONLY subtournament S with ω̄(S) ≥ 4 is the whole tournament (S = the full vertex set). So no subtournament strictly smaller than T attains ω̄ ≥ 4; since T can be made arbitrarily large, there is no bounded-size witness ℓ(4), refuting Question 5.9 at k = 4.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes

question_5_9_fails_at_k5.title: AACL Question 5.9 fails at k = 5
question_5_9_fails_at_k5.decoded: For every L there exists a tournament T that is 5-ω̄-critical, has more than L vertices, and whose ONLY subtournament S with ω̄ ≥ 5 is the whole tournament (S = the full vertex set). So there is no bound ℓ(5): one cannot witness ω̄ ≥ 5 inside a subtournament of bounded size, which is exactly what Question 5.9 asks for at k = 5 — hence Question 5.9 (the existence of such a bounding function ℓ) is NOT the case at k = 5.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (Clique number of tournaments, 2023)

question_5_9_statement.title: AACL Question 5.9 — compact ω̄-cluster (f = id form)
question_5_9_statement.decoded: There exists a single function ℓ : ℕ → ℕ such that for every tournament T and every k, if ω̄(T) ≥ k then T has a vertex subset S of size at most ℓ(k) whose induced subtournament already satisfies ω̄(sub_tournament S) ≥ k. This is the f = id (threshold exactly k) strengthening of Conjecture 5.8; Conjecture 5.10 refutes it.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (2023)

rainbow_triangle_c3head.title: C3 has a rainbow directed triangle under a head-index colouring
rainbow_triangle_c3head.decoded: Colouring each arc by its head's index gives C3's three cyclic arcs three distinct colours, so rainbow_triangle is satisfiable and the left disjunct of mono_reach_or_rainbow_statement is realisable.

sands_sauer_woodrow_statement.title: Sands–Sauer–Woodrow monochromatic reachability (arc-coloured digraphs)
sands_sauer_woodrow_statement.decoded: For every number of colours k there is a bound f such that every k-arc-coloured digraph D has a vertex set S which is a union of at most f stable sets and such that every vertex v of D reaches some vertex of S by a monochromatic directed path (a path whose arcs all carry one colour; the colour and target may depend on v).

sdeg_le1_is_forest.title: A simple graph of maximum degree ≤ 1 is a forest
sdeg_le1_is_forest.decoded: Proves the reusable structural fact that any simple graph whose every vertex has degree at most 1 (a matching plus isolated vertices) is a forest: any two irredundant paths with the same endpoints coincide. This is the engine behind linear_forest back-arc-graph arguments and tests the is_forest / linear_forest primitives.

seymour_second_neighbourhood_statement.title: Seymour's Second Neighbourhood Conjecture
seymour_second_neighbourhood_statement.decoded: Every nonempty oriented (digon-free) digraph D (0 < #|D|) has a "Seymour vertex": there exists a vertex v whose first out-neighbourhood is no larger than its second out-neighbourhood, i.e. outdeg(v) ≤ #|Nout2(v)|, where Nout2(v) is the set of vertices reachable from v by a directed path of length exactly 2 (and not already out-neighbours of v).

splitting_min_outdegree_statement.title: Splitting a digraph with minimum out-degree constraints (Alon; Thomassen)
splitting_min_outdegree_statement.decoded: There is a function f : nat → nat such that for every digraph D and every d, if every vertex has out-degree at least f(d), then the vertex set splits into a proper bipartition (V1 nonempty and not all of D, with complement ~:V1) in which each part induces minimum out-degree at least d — every v in V1 has outdeg_in(V1, v) ≥ d, and every v outside V1 has outdeg_in(~:V1, v) ≥ d, where outdeg_in(A,v) counts only arcs whose head lies in A.
splitting_min_outdegree_statement.match_title: Splitting a digraph with minimum outdegree constraints

ssw_class_inhabited.title: Non-vacuity: a k-arc-coloured digraph exists for every k ≥ 1
ssw_class_inhabited.decoded: For every k ≥ 1 exhibits C3 with a constant k-arc-colouring, so the antecedent class of sands_sauer_woodrow_statement is inhabited; the quantification over k-arc-coloured digraphs is not vacuous (k = 0 is correctly degenerate since 'I_0 admits no colour).

stable_meeting_longest_dipaths_statement.title: Stable set meeting all longest directed paths (Laborde–Payan–Xuong)
stable_meeting_longest_dipaths_statement.decoded: Every digraph D (no nonemptiness guard) has a stable set S (an independent set: no two of its vertices are joined by an arc in either direction) that meets every longest directed path — for every directed path x :: s whose LENGTH (number of arcs, size s) attains the maximum ell(D) = max over dipaths of size s (so the path has ell(D) arcs and ell(D)+1 vertices, NOT ell(D) vertices), some vertex of S lies on that path (v ∈ x :: s).
stable_meeting_longest_dipaths_statement.match_title: Stable set meeting all longest directed paths

symcyc3_in_H2.title: The symmetric 3-cycle is a base member of H₂
symcyc3_in_H2.decoded: Proves the symmetric triangle sym_cycle 3 inhabits [in_H2_concrete] (for any realisation) via the inH2_base constructor, validating that the H₂ base class of the conj_9_2 conjectures is non-empty.

symcyc3_outdeg0.title: Each symmetric-3-cycle vertex has out-degree 2 (discriminating value)
symcyc3_outdeg0.decoded: Computes the out-degree of a sym_cycle 3 vertex as exactly 2 (two reciprocal neighbours), distinguishing the symmetric triangle from the directed rim di_cycle (out-degree 1). A discriminating value check on the base block; symcyc3_Eulerian similarly grounds in = out = 2.

symcyc3_strong.title: The bidirected triangle is strongly connected
symcyc3_strong.decoded: Verifies strongb (sym_cycle 3) by exhibiting connectivity on every ordered pair, grounding the textbook fact that a bidirected cycle is strong — a required property of the H₂ base members.

symmetric_odd_cycle_inhabited.title: The symmetric triangle is a symmetric odd cycle
symmetric_odd_cycle_inhabited.decoded: Proves symmetric_odd_cycle (sym_cycle 3), so the base class of the family H₂ (the symmetric odd cycles) is non-vacuous — without an inhabitant the entire base of the H₂ construction would say nothing.

tournament_indeg_outdeg.title: Tournament degree identity: indeg v + outdeg v = n - 1
tournament_indeg_outdeg.decoded: Proves the textbook tournament degree identity — in any tournament on n vertices, every vertex v satisfies indeg v + outdeg v = n - 1, since every other vertex is joined to v by exactly one arc. This validates that the new indeg / outdeg (Nout / Nin) primitives partition the non-v vertices correctly, the foundation on which the Seymour and Caccetta-Häggkvist statements are built.

transitive_tournament_TT.title: Every TT n is a transitive tournament (dichotomy RHS non-vacuity)
transitive_tournament_TT.decoded: Proves every TT n satisfies [transitive_tournament] (semicomplete + asymmetric + irreflexive with transitive arc = <). Grounds that the 'transitive tournament' disjunct of the hero-dichotomy RHS is inhabited.

transitive_tournament_TT'.title: Every TT l is a transitive tournament
transitive_tournament_TT'.decoded: Proves each transitive tournament TT l is genuinely a transitive tournament (irreflexive, total, antisymmetric, transitive), inhabiting the right disjunct of the hero dichotomy (conj_4_2) and the no-induced-Kₗ / hero antecedent class.

tvec_core_statement.title: Maximum dichromatic number of oriented triangle-free graphs — combinatorial core (t⃗)
tvec_core_statement.decoded: There is a sequence h : nat -> nat such that, for every order n > 0: (upper bound) every digon-free digraph D on exactly n vertices with triangle-free underlying graph is h(n)-dicolourable (χ⃗(D) ≤ h(n)); and (attainment) some such order-n graph is NOT (h(n)−1)-dicolourable, pinning h(n) to the exact maximum dichromatic number t⃗(n). This is the combinatorial core of the Θ(√(n/log n)) conjecture, with the asymptotic envelope abstracted into the existence of such a binding sequence h.

source.name: arXiv 2403.02298
source.attribution: Aboulker, Bousquet, de Verclos et al.

twinwidth_chain_consistent.title: Twin-width chain 3.16 ⇒ 3.13 ⇒ 3.16 ⇒ 3.12 is consistent with the direct edge
twinwidth_chain_consistent.decoded: Composes the committed twin-width implication edges and confirms that the composite route 3.16⇒3.13⇒3.16⇒3.12 lands on the same conj_3_12 target as the direct 3.16⇒3.12 edge, so the edges are transitive and never contradict.

two_extremal_card_pos.title: A two-extremal digraph has positive order (non-degeneracy)
two_extremal_card_pos.decoded: Proves any two_extremal digraph has at least one vertex, so the conj_9_2 quantifier is not over the empty digraph. A non-degeneracy guard against the vacuously-false reading that caused prior bugs.

two_extremal_is_four_conjuncts.title: two_extremal unfolds to its four intended conjuncts
two_extremal_is_four_conjuncts.decoded: Exposes that [two_extremal] is exactly the 4-way conjunction strong + 2-connected underlying + arc-connectivity 2 + χ⃗ = 3. A faithfulness datum: a mis-encoding that silently dropped a conjunct would fail to typecheck against this statement.

tww_le_TT1.title: The one-vertex tournament has twin-width 0
tww_le_TT1.decoded: Computes that the concrete one-vertex tournament TT 1 satisfies tww_le (TT 1) 0, validating tww_le as a non-vacuous predicate with an explicit witness (the textbook fact that graphs on at most one vertex have twin-width 0). tww_le is the antecedent only of Conjecture 3.12.

tww_le_TT1_all.title: TT 1 lies in every twin-width class {tww ≤ k}
tww_le_TT1_all.decoded: Shows tww_le (TT 1) k holds for every k, so the bounded-twin-width antecedent tww_le T k of Conjecture 3.12 has a concrete inhabitant at every width bound k — the for-all is not over an empty class.

tww_le_mono.title: Twin-width bound tww_le is monotone in k
tww_le_mono.decoded: Proves tww_le D k implies tww_le D k+1 by reusing the same contraction-sequence witness with a looser bound. This validates the shape of the twin-width 'at most k' predicate: a real minimum-width bound must be upward closed in k.

unvd_K1.title: The unavoidability number of a single vertex is exactly 1
unvd_K1.decoded: Computes unvd(K₁) = 1: every 1-vertex tournament contains the single-vertex digraph K₁, and the value 0 fails because the empty tournament TT 0 contains no vertex. A concrete value witness grounding the unvd (least-N-unavoidable) definition behind Problem 6.

unvd_K2.title: The unavoidability number of a single arc is exactly 2
unvd_K2.decoded: Computes unvd(K₂) = 2 for the single-arc digraph: every 2-vertex tournament is an arc (so contains K₂), while no tournament on fewer than 2 vertices does. A second concrete value witness grounding the unvd definition of Problem 6.

unvd_relation_inhabited.title: The unvd relation is satisfiable
unvd_relation_inhabited.decoded: Proves unvd K1 1 holds: a concrete (digraph, value) pair satisfies the unvd relation (a least-unavoidable value exists for a nonempty digraph). Confirms the unvd-based conjectures range over a non-empty relation, not an empty graph of pairs.

vglue_singletons_card.title: Vertex-gluing two singletons collapses to one vertex
vglue_singletons_card.decoded: Computes #|vglue o1 o1| = 1: the categorical-pushout vertex amalgam vglue of two single-vertex digraphs identifies the two chosen vertices, collapsing the 2-vertex sum to one class. Grounds that the vglue amalgam really merges as intended.

vt_kcritical.title: k-ω̄-criticality of a vertex-transitive tournament reduces to one deletion
vt_kcritical.decoded: For a vertex-transitive tournament T (vertex_transitiveb T) and any fixed vertex v₀, T is k-ω̄-critical if and only if ω̄(T) = k and ω̄(del_tournament v₀) = k−1. Vertex-transitivity makes all single-vertex deletions interchangeable, so the universally-quantified deletion test in the definition of kcritical collapses to a single arbitrary deletion at v₀.

source.name: arXiv 2310.04265
source.attribution: Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Lopes (2023)

wheel_tree3_even_B_parity.title: The wheel hub star satisfies even-B-parity
wheel_tree3_even_B_parity.decoded: Proves the star hub tree wheel_tree 3 has even-B-parity (every leaf-to-leaf path crosses an even number of B-edges). A second independent witness, beyond canon_tree, that the Def-9.1 parity legality predicate is satisfiable on a non-trivial tree.

woodall_empty_ok.title: Woodall is not refutable on the empty digraph
woodall_empty_ok.decoded: Shows the Woodall antecedent (a one-way set of a given dicut size) is unsatisfiable on the empty digraph TT 0, so the implication holds vacuously there. Confirms Woodall has no empty-digraph counterexample (the bug class that earlier hit Bermond-Thomassen / Hoang-Reed).

woodall_statement.title: Woodall's Conjecture (directed Lucchesi–Younger)
woodall_statement.decoded: For every digraph D and every k, if the minimum dicut size is k — encoded as: some one-way set B (nonempty proper set with no arc entering it) has dicut size exactly k, and every one-way set has dicut size at least k — then there exist k dijoins (arc selectors each meeting the dicut of every one-way set) that are pairwise arc-disjoint (all dijoin Js, size Js = k, and for all i ≠ j the i-th and j-th selectors share no arc). I.e. the minimum dicut size equals the maximum number of arc-disjoint dijoins.
